broadcaster: stream results to connected surfaces.\n\nManages SSE connections and broadcasts events to relevant surfaces.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport os\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Dict\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\n# Idle-stream keepalive. An SSE connection that sends nothing while it waits for\n# events is indistinguishable from a dead one: the browser's EventSource only\n# fires ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail, so a *silent* network drop on an\n# idle stream leaves the surface showing \"Connected\" indefinitely (until the\n# OS-level TCP keepalive times out, many minutes later). Emitting a comment\n# line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``) every few seconds keeps traffic flowing, so:\n#\n#   - proxies/load balancers won't reap the connection as idle, and\n#   - the next ping after a real network drop fails to arrive, the client read\n#     errors, ``onerror`` fires, and the native EventSource reconnects — which\n#     is exactly the resync path the canvas relies on.\n#\n# Comment lines are ignored by EventSource (they never surface as messages), so\n# this adds no client-visible events. ``5s`` is frequent enough that a drop is\n# surfaced well inside any realistic reconnect budget, and quiet enough not to\n# be chatty. Lower it (e.g. via ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS) for faster\n# reconnection tests.\nKEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = float(\n    os.environ.get(\"ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS\", \"5\")\n)\n\n# Sentinel pushed onto a connection's queue by :meth:`SSEBroadcaster.drop_session`\n# to make that connection's :meth:`event_generator` return ABRUPTLY — i.e. without\n# emitting a ``disconnect`` event. An abrupt end of the response body is what the\n# browser's ``EventSource`` treats as a dropped connection: it fires ``onerror``\n# and performs its NATIVE auto-reconnect. (The graceful ``disconnect`` event, by\n# contrast, makes the client call ``close()`` and stay down.) This is the only\n# way to faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop from a test\n# against a loopback server — Playwright ``context.set_offline`` cannot break an\n# already-established loopback SSE stream (see tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n_DROP = object()\n\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass SSEConnection:\n    \"\"\"An active SSE connection.\"\"\"\n    connection_id: str\n    surface_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    surface_type: str\n    queue: asyncio.Queue = field(default_factory=asyncio.Queue)\n    connected_at: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n    last_heartbeat: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass SSEEvent:\n    \"\"\"An SSE event to broadcast.\n\n    Attributes:\n        event_type: The type of SSE event (e.g., 'result_created', 'topic_updated')\n        data: The event payload data\n        rendered_html: Optional rendered HTML for canvas injection (e.g., pre-rendered card)\n        target_session_id: Optional filter to only send to connections for this session\n        target_surface_id: Optional filter to only send to this specific surface\n        exclude_surface_id: Optional filter to exclude this surface from receiving the event\n    \"\"\"\n    event_type: str\n    data: dict\n    rendered_html: str | None = None\n    target_session_id: str | None = None\n    target_surface_id: str | None = None\n    exclude_surface_id: str | None = None\n\n\nclass SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"\n    Manages SSE connections and broadcasts events.\n\n    Events are queued per-connection and sent via SSE.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.connections: Dict[str, SSEConnection] = {}\n        self._cleanup_task: asyncio.Task | None = None\n        self._running = False\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n        self._running = True\n        self._cleanup_task = asyncio.create_task(self._cleanup_loop())\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n        self._running = False\n        if self._cleanup_task:\n            self._cleanup_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await self._cleanup_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n    def register(\n        self,\n        surface_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n    ) -> SSEConnection:\n        \"\"\"Register a new SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        connection_id = str(uuid4())\n        connection = SSEConnection(\n            connection_id=connection_id,\n            surface_id=surface_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            surface_type=surface_type,\n        )\n        self.connections[connection_id] = connection\n        logger.info(f\"Registered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {surface_id}\")\n        return connection\n\n    def unregister(self, connection_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Unregister an SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            conn = self.connections[connection_id]\n            logger.info(f\"Unregistered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {conn.surface_id}\")\n            del self.connections[connection_id]\n\n    def drop_session(self, session_id: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Abruptly drop every live SSE stream for ``session_id``.\n\n        Pushes the ``_DROP`` sentinel onto each matching connection's queue so\n        its :meth:`event_generator` returns without emitting a ``disconnect``\n        event — the browser's ``EventSource`` then sees the stream end abruptly,\n        fires ``onerror``, and performs its native auto-reconnect. Returns the\n        number of streams signalled. Used by ``POST /api/v1/test/drop-sse`` to\n        faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop, which\n        ``context.set_offline`` cannot reproduce against a loopback server\n        (loopback connections are exempt from offline emulation — see\n        tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n        \"\"\"\n        count = 0\n        for conn in self.connections.values():\n            if conn.session_id == session_id:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(_DROP)\n                count += 1\n        if count:\n            logger.info(f\"Drop requested for session {session_id}: {count} stream(s)\")\n        return count\n\n    def heartbeat(self, connection_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Update heartbeat for a connection. Returns True if connection exists.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            self.connections[connection_id].last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    async def broadcast(self, event: SSEEvent) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Broadcast an event to relevant connections.\n\n        Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n        \"\"\"\n        sent_count = 0\n\n        for conn in list(self.connections.values()):\n            # Filter by target\n            if event.target_session_id and conn.session_id != event.target_session_id:\n                continue\n            if event.target_surface_id and conn.surface_id != event.target_surface_id:\n                continue\n            if event.exclude_surface_id and conn.surface_id == event.exclude_surface_id:\n                continue\n\n            # Queue the event\n            try:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(event)\n                sent_count += 1\n            except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                logger.warning(f\"Queue full for connection {conn.connection_id}, dropping event\")\n\n        return sent_count\n\n    async def event_generator(self, connection: SSEConnection):\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate SSE events for a connection.\n\n        Yields formatted SSE messages.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event\n            yield self._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"connection_id\": connection.connection_id,\n                \"surface_id\": connection.surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": connection.session_id,\n            })\n\n            while True:\n                # Block on the next event, but no longer than the keepalive\n                # interval: if nothing arrives, emit a comment-line ping so the\n                # stream is never silent. A silent idle stream is indistinguish-\n                # able from a dead one to the browser — EventSource only fires\n                # ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail — so without these pings\n                # a real network drop on an idle surface leaves it showing\n                # \"Connected\" until the OS TCP keepalive times out (minutes\n                # later). The ping is a comment line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``), which\n                # EventSource ignores — it surfaces no message to the client.\n                try:\n                    event = await asyncio.wait_for(\n                        connection.queue.get(),\n                        timeout=KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS,\n                    )\n                except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                    yield \": ping\\n\\n\"\n                    continue\n\n                # Abrupt drop sentinel (see _DROP): end the stream WITHOUT a\n                # disconnect event so the browser EventSource onerrors + reconnects.\n                if event is _DROP:\n                    logger.info(f\"Drop sentinel on connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    return\n\n                connection.last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n\n                # Format and yield the event\n                payload = dict(event.data)\n                if event.rendered_html is not None:\n                    payload[\"rendered_html\"] = event.rendered_html\n                yield self._format_sse(event.event_type, payload)\n\n                # Special case: disconnect event ends the stream\n                if event.event_type == \"disconnect\":\n                    logger.info(f\"Disconnect event for connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    break\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"Connection {connection.connection_id} cancelled\")\n            raise\n        finally:\n            self.unregister(connection.connection_id)\n\n    def _format_sse(self, event_type: str, data: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format an event as SSE.\"\"\"\n        return f\"event: {event_type}\\ndata: {json.dumps(data)}\\n\\n\"\n\n    async def _cleanup_loop(self):\n        \"\"\"Periodically clean up dead connections.\"\"\"\n        while self._running:\n            try:\n                await asyncio.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds\n\n                now = datetime.now().timestamp()\n                timeout = 300  # 5 minutes\n\n                dead_connections = [\n                    cid for cid, conn in self.connections.items()\n                    if (now - conn.last_heartbeat) > timeout\n                ]\n\n                for cid in dead_connections:\n                    logger.info(f\"Cleaning up dead connection {cid}\")\n                    # Send disconnect event before removing\n                    try:\n                        conn = self.connections[cid]\n                        conn.queue.put_nowait(SSEEvent(\n                            event_type=\"disconnect\",\n                            data={\"reason\": \"timeout\"},\n                        ))\n                    except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                        pass\n                    self.unregister(cid)\n\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in cleanup loop: {e}\")\n\n\n# Global broadcaster instance\n_broadcaster: SSEBroadcaster | None = None\n\n\ndef get_broadcaster() -> SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global SSE broadcaster.\"\"\"\n    global _broadcaster\n    if _broadcaster is None:\n        _broadcaster = SSEBroadcaster()\n    return _broadcaster\n\n\n# Event types\nclass EventType:\n    \"\"\"SSE event types.\"\"\"\n\n    # Connection lifecycle\n    CONNECTED = \"connected\"\n    DISCONNECT = \"disconnect\"\n    HEARTBEAT = \"heartbeat\"\n\n    # Result events\n    RESULT_CREATED = \"result_created\"\n    RESULT_UPDATED = \"result_updated\"\n\n    # Component events (Phase 2)\n    COMPONENT_UPDATED = \"component_updated\"\n\n    # Intent events\n    INTENT_PENDING = \"intent_pending\"\n    INTENT_DISPATCHED = \"intent_dispatched\"\n    INTENT_RESOLVED = \"intent_resolved\"\n\n    # Topic events\n    TOPIC_CREATED = \"topic_created\"\n    TOPIC_UPDATED = \"topic_updated\"\n    TOPIC_STALE = \"topic_stale\"\n\n    # Workload events\n    WORKLOAD_SUMMARY = \"workload_summary\"\n    EXCEPTION_RAISED = \"exception_raised\"\n\n    # Bead events\n    BEAD_CLOSED = \"bead_closed\"\n    BEAD_FAILED = \"bead_failed\"\n\n    # Circuit breaker events\n    TASK_STUCK = \"task_stuck\"\n    TASK_FAILED = \"task_failed\"\n\n\nasync def broadcast_result(\n    result: dict,\n    session_id: str,\n    target_surface_id: str | None = None,\n    rendered_html: str | None = None,\n) -> int:\n    \"\"\"\n    Broadcast a result to relevant surfaces.\n\n    Args:\n        result: The result data payload\n        session_id: The session ID to target\n        target_surface_id: Optional specific surface to target\n        rendered_html: Optional pre-rendered HTML for canvas injection\n\n    Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    event = SSEEvent(\n        event_type=EventType.RESULT_CREATED,\n        data=result,\n        rendered_html=rendered_html,\n        target_session_id=session_id,\n        target_surface_id=target_surface_id,\n    )\n    return await broadcaster.broadcast(event)\n\n\nasync def broadcast_intent_update(\n    intent: dict,\n    session_id: str,\n    event_type: str = EventType.INTENT_RESOLVED,\n) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Broadcast an intent status update.\"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    event = SSEEvent(\n        event_type=event_type,\n        data=intent,\n        target_session_id=session_id,\n    )\n    return await broadcaster.broadcast(event)\n\n\nasync def broadcast_workload_summary(\n    session_id: str,\n    summary: dict,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Broadcast a workload summary (for reconnection).\"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    event = SSEEvent(\n        event_type=EventType.WORKLOAD_SUMMARY,\n        data=summary,\n        target_session_id=session_id,\n        target_surface_id=surface_id,\n    )\n    return await broadcaster.broadcast(event)\n","numLines":394,"startLine":1,"totalLines":394}}}

_evaluate_filter(self, state_data: dict, filter_expr: str) -> bool:\n237\t        \"\"\"\n238\t        Evaluate a single filter expression against state data.\n239\t\n240\t        Supports: !=, >, <, >=, <=, ==\n241\t        Examples: \"phase!=Running\", \"restarts>0\", \"ready!=1/1\"\n242\t        \"\"\"\n243\t        import re\n244\t\n245\t        # Parse filter expression\n246\t        match = True\n250\t\n251\t        field_path, op, value = match.groups()\n252\t\n253\t        # Get field value from nested dict\n254\t        field_value = self._get_nested_value(state_data, float(field_value) >= float(value)\n271\t            elif op == \"<=\":\n272\t                return float(field_value) <= float(value)\n273\t        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n274\t            # If comparison fails, treat as no match\n275\t            logger.warning(f\"Filter comparison failed: {filter_expr} with value {field_value}\")\n276\t            return False\n277\t\n278\t current state.\"\"\"\n459\t        diff = {}\n460\t\n461\t        for key in set(list(previous.keys()) + list(current.keys())):\n462\t            prev_val = previous.get(key)\n463\t def evaluate_exception_rules(\n509\t        self,\n510\t        changes_dict: dict,\n511\t        project_slug: str,\n512\t        rules: list[ExceptionRule],\n513\t    ) -> list[dict]:\n514\t        \"\"\"\n515\t        Evaluate exception rules against state changes.\n516\t\n517\t        Returns list of triggered rule outputs with project_slug, urgency, and exception type.\n518\t        Rules           return []\n523\t\n524\t        diff = changes_dict.get(\"diff\", {})\n525\t        triggered_rules = []\n526\t\n527\t        for rule in rules:\n528\t            # Check if rule applies to _rule_condition_matches(self, condition: str, diff: dict) -> bool:\n546\t        \"\"\"\n547\t        Check if a rule condition matches the diff.\n548\t\n549\t        Conditions are simple field comparisons against the diff.\n550\t        Examples:\n551\t        - \"phase==Failed\" -> true if phase changed to Failed\n552\t        - \"restarts>5\" -> true if restarts changed to value > 5\n553\t        - \"sync_status==OutOfSync\" -> true if sync_status changed to OutOfSync\n554\t        \"\"\"\n555\t        import re\n556\t\n557\t        # Parse condition\n558\t        match = re.match(r'(\\w+)(==|!=|>=|<=|>|<)(.+)', condition)\n559\t        if not match:\n560\t            logger.warning(f\"Invalid rule condition: {condition}\")\n561\t            return False\n562\t\n563\t new value (what it changed \"to\")\n570\t        new_value = diff[field].get(\"to\")\n571\t\n572\t        if new_value is None:\n573\t            return False\n574\t\n575\t        # Evaluate condition\n576\t        try:\n577\t            if op == \"==\":\n578\t                return str(new_value) == expected_value\n579\t            elif op == \"!=\":\n580\t                return str(new_value) != expected_value\n581\t            elif op == \">\":\n582\t                return float(new_value) > float(expected_value)\n583\t            elif op == \"<\":\n584\t                return float(new_value) < float(expected_value)\n585\t            elif op == \">=\":\n586\t                return float(new_value) >= float(expected_value)\n587\t            elif op == \"<=\":\n588\t                return float(new_value) <= float(expected_value)\n589\t        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n590\t            logger.warning(f\"Rule condition evaluation failed: {condition} with value {new_value}\")\n591\t            return False\n592\t\n593\t        return False\n594\t\n595\t    async def monitor_topic(self, rule: MonitoringRule) -> None:\n596\t        \"\"\"\n597\t          urgency=item.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                filters=item.get(\"filters\", []),\n                notification_threshold=item.get(\"notification_threshold\", \"any_change\"),\n      name=item[\"name\"],\n                project_slug=item.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                condition=item[\"condition\"],\n                urgency=item.get(\"urgency\", \"high\"),\n                message=item[\"message\"],\n            ))\n\n        # Parse batching\n        batching = {\n            \"low_urgency_batch_seconds\": data.get(\"batching\", {}).get(\"low_urgency_batch_seconds\", 300),\n            \"normal_urgency_batch_seconds\": data.get(\"batching\", {}).get(\"normal_urgency_batch_seconds\", 120),\n        }\n\n        # Parse quiet hours\n        quiet_hours = data.get(\"quiet_hours\", {})\n\n        # Parse channels\n        channels = data.get(\"channels\", {})\n\n        self.config = MonitoringConfig(\n            active_topics=active_topics,\n            exceptions=exceptions,\n            batching=batching,\n            quiet_hours=quiet_hours,\n            channels=channels,\n        )\n        return self.config\n\n    async def check_topic_state(self, rule: MonitoringRule) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check the current state of a topic.\n\n        Uses the fetch orchestrator with the full command matrix to get current state.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map monitoring intent_type to fetch IntentType\n        intent_type = INTENT_TYPE_MAPPING.get(rule.intent_type, IntentType.STATUS)\n\n        # Build fetch context\n        context = FetchContext(\n            project_slug=rule.project_slug,\n            namespace=rule.project_slug.replace(\"-\", \"\") if rule.project_slug else None,\n            repo_path=f\"/home/coding/{rule.project_slug}\" if rule.project_slug else None,\n            app_name=rule.project_slug,\n            deployment=rule.project_slug,\n        )\n\n        # Create fetch request\n        request = FetchRequest(\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n            context=context,\n            intent_id=f\"monitoring-{rule.topic_id}\",\n            session_id=\"monitoring\",\n        )\n\n        # Execute fetch using the orchestrator (uses full fetch command matrix)\n        try:\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(request)\n\n            # Extract successful source data\n            successful_data = fetch_result.get_successful_data()\n\n            if not successful_data:\n                logger.warning(f\"No successful sources for {rule.topic_id}\")\n                return None\n\n            # Build state data from all successful sources\n            state_data = {\n                \"project_slug\": rule.project_slug,\n                \"intent_type\": rule.intent_type,\n                \"sources\": list(successful_data.keys()),\n                **{k: v for source in successful_data for k, v in successful_data[source].items()},\n            }\n\n            # Apply filters if specified\n            if rule.filters:\n                if not self._passes_filters(state_data, rule.filters):\n                    # Filter means we don't report this state\n                    logger.debug(f\"State for {rule.topic_id} filtered by {rule.filters}\")\n                    return None\n\n            logger.debug(f\"Fetched state for {rule.topic_id} from {len(successful_data)} sources\")\n            return state_data\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Error checking state for {rule.topic_id}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n            return None\n\n    def _passes_filters(self, state_data: dict, filters: list[str]) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if state data passes all filters.\n\n        Filters are simple expressions like \"phase!=Running\" or \"restarts>0\".\n        Returns False if any filter fails.\n        \"\"\"\n        for filter_expr in filters:\n            if not self._evaluate_filter(state_data, filter_expr):\n                return False\n        return True\n\n    def _evaluate_filter(self, state_data: dict, filter_expr: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate a single filter expression against state data.\n\n        Supports: !=, >, <, >=, <=, ==\n        Examples: \"phase!=Running\", \"restarts>0\", \"ready!=1/1\"\n        \"\"\"\n        import re\n\n        # Parse filter expression\n        match = re.match(r'(\\w+)(!=|>=|<=|>|<|==)(.+)', filter_expr)\n        if not match:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid filter expression: {filter_expr}\")\n            return True\n\n        field_path, op, value = match.groups()\n\n        # Get field value from nested dict\n        field_value = self._get_nested_value(state_data, field_path)\n\n        # Compare\n        if field_value is None:\n            return False\n\n        try:\n            if op == \"!=\":\n                return str(field_value) != value\n            elif op == \"==\":\n                return str(field_value) == value\n            elif op == \">\":\n                return float(field_value) > float(value)\n            elif op == \"<\":\n                return float(field_value) < float(value)\n            elif op == \">=\":\n                return float(field_value) >= float(value)\n            elif op == \"<=\":\n                return float(field_value) <= float(value)\n        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n            # If comparison fails, treat as no match\n            logger.warning(f\"Filter comparison failed: {filter_expr} with value {field_value}\")\n            return False\n\n        return True\n\n    def _get_nested_value(self, data: dict, path: str) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Get a value from nested dict using dot notation.\"\"\"\n        keys = path.split(\".\")\n        value = data\n        for key in keys:\n            if isinstance(value, dict):\n                value = value.get(key)\n            else:\n                return None\n        return value\n\n    async def _get_topic_context_cache(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get cached context data for a topic from topic_context_cache table.\"\"\"\n        cached = await self._store.get_topic_context(topic_id)\n        if cached:\n            return cached.get(\"context\")\n        return None\n\n    async def _update_topic_context_cache(\n        self,\n        topic_id: str,\n        context_data: dict,\n        ttl_seconds: int = TOPIC_CONTEXT_TTL_SECONDS,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Store context data in topic_context_cache table.\"\"\"\n        await self._store.set_topic_context(\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            context_data=context_data,\n            ttl_seconds=ttl_seconds,\n        )\n\n    async def detect_state_change(\n        self,\n        rule: MonitoringRule,\n        current_state: dict,\n    ) -> tuple[bool, dict]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Detect if the state has changed since last check.\n\n        Reads from topic_context_cache to get previous state.\n        Returns (has_change, changes_dict) where changes_dict tracks what changed.\n\n        The changes_dict includes:\n        - 'changed_fields': list of field names that changed\n        - 'diff': full diff between previous and current state\n        - 'is_first': True if this is the first check (no previous state)\n        \"\"\"\n        # Get cached context from database\n        cached_context = await self._get_topic_context_cache(rule.topic_id)\n\n        if cached_context is None:\n            # First check - no previous state in cache yet\n            # Store current state as baseline and don't notify\n            await self._update_topic_context_cache(rule.topic_id, current_state)\n            return False, {\n                \"is_first\": True,\n                \"changed_fields\": [],\n                \"diff\": {},\n            }\n\n        # Compute diff between previous and current state\n        diff = self._compute_diff(cached_context, current_state)\n        changed_fields = list(diff.keys())\n\n        # Check for state changes based on notification threshold\n        if rule.notification_threshold == \"any_change\":\n            # Any field change triggers notification\n            has_change = len(changed_fields) > 0\n        elif rule.notification_threshold == \"state_change\":\n            # Only notify if specific state fields change\n            # For status intents, check if phase, status, or health changed\n            state_fields = [\"phase\", \"status\", \"health\", \"ready\", \"sync_status\", \"sync_status\"]\n            significant_changes = [f for f in changed_fields if f in state_fields]\n            has_change = len(significant_changes) > 0\n        else:\n            has_change = False\n\n        return has_change, {\n            \"is_first\": False,\n            \"changed_fields\": changed_fields,\n            \"diff\": diff,\n        }\n\n    async def push_monitoring_result(\n        self,\n        rule: MonitoringRule,\n        current_state: dict,\n        changes_dict: dict,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Push a monitoring result to the active surface.\n\n        Creates a result in the session store with intent_id=NULL (system-originated).\n        Reads previous state from topic_context_cache and updates it after writing result.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Find or create topic for this monitoring rule\n        topic_id, _ = await self._store.find_or_create_topic(\n            label=rule.topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            topic_type=\"project\",\n            project_slugs=[rule.project_slug] if rule.project_slug else None,\n        )\n\n        # Get previous state from cache\n        previous_state = await self._get_topic_context_cache(rule.topic_id)\n\n        result_data = {\n            \"monitoring\": True,\n            \"current_state\": current_state,\n            \"previous_state\": previous_state,\n            \"changed_fields\": changes_dict.get(\"changed_fields\", []),\n            \"diff\": changes_dict.get(\"diff\", {}),\n            \"is_first_check\": changes_dict.get(\"is_first\", False),\n            \"rule_filters\": rule.filters,\n            \"notification_threshold\": rule.notification_threshold,\n            \"triggered_rules\": changes_dict.get(\"triggered_rules\", []),\n        }\n\n        # Derive result_type from monitoring intent type per plan §10\n        result_type = derive_result_type(intent_type=\"monitoring\", project_slug=rule.project_slug)\n\n        # Write result with intent_id=NULL (system-originated, no utterance behind it)\n        result_id = await self._store.create_result(\n            intent_id=None,  # NULL for monitoring-originated results\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            summary=self._generate_summary(rule, current_state, previous_state, changes_dict),\n            data=result_data,\n            urgency=rule.urgency,\n            result_type=result_type,\n        )\n\n        # Render monitoring card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n        renderer = get_renderer()\n        summary_text = self._generate_summary(rule, current_state, previous_state, changes_dict)\n        render_outcome = renderer.render(\n            result_id=result_id,\n            result_type=result_type,\n            result_data=result_data,\n            summary=summary_text,\n            urgency=rule.urgency,\n        )\n\n        # Update result's card_fallback flag\n        await self._store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Created monitoring result {result_id} for topic {rule.topic_id} (intent_id=NULL)\")\n\n        # Fire SSE event for canvas update\n        # Build result dict for broadcast\n        result_for_broadcast = {\n            \"id\": result_id,\n            \"intent_id\": None,\n            \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n            \"session_id\": session_id,\n            \"summary\": summary_text,\n            \"data\": result_data,\n            \"urgency\": rule.urgency,\n            \"result_type\": result_type,\n            \"card_fallback\": render_outcome.card_fallback,\n            \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n            \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        # Broadcast to all surfaces in the session\n        await broadcast_result(\n            result=result_for_broadcast,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(f\"Broadcast SSE event for monitoring result {result_id}\")\n\n        # Update topic context cache with current state\n        await self._update_topic_context_cache(rule.topic_id, current_state)\n\n    def _compute_diff(self, previous: dict, current: dict) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Compute diff between previous and current state.\"\"\"\n        diff = {}\n\n        for key in set(list(previous.keys()) + list(current.keys())):\n            prev_val = previous.get(key)\n            curr_val = current.get(key)\n\n            if prev_val != curr_val:\n                diff[key] = {\n                    \"from\": prev_val,\n                    \"to\": curr_val,\n                }\n\n        return diff\n\n    def _generate_summary(\n        self,\n        rule: MonitoringRule,\n        current_state: dict,\n        previous_state: Optional[dict],\n        changes_dict: dict,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a summary for the monitoring result.\"\"\"\n        if changes_dict.get(\"is_first\"):\n            return f\"Initial state check for {rule.topic_id}\"\n\n        changed_fields = changes_dict.get(\"changed_fields\", [])\n        diff = changes_dict.get(\"diff\", {})\n\n        # Check for specific state changes\n        if \"phase\" in diff:\n            prev_phase = diff[\"phase\"].get(\"from\")\n            curr_phase = diff[\"phase\"].get(\"to\")\n            return f\"{rule.topic_id} phase changed from {prev_phase} to {curr_phase}\"\n\n        if \"sync_status\" in diff:\n            prev_sync = diff[\"sync_status\"].get(\"from\")\n            curr_sync = diff[\"sync_status\"].get(\"to\")\n            return curr_health = diff[\"health_status\"].get(\"to\")\n            return f\"{rule.topic_id} health status changed from {prev_health} to {curr_health}\"\n\n        if changed_fields:\n            return f\"{rule.topic_id} changed: {', '.join(changed_fields[:3])}\" + (\"...\" if len(changed_fields) > 3 else \"\")\n\n        return f\"{rule.topic_id} state updated\"\n\n    def evaluate_exception_rules(\n        self,\n        changes_dict: dict,\n        project_slug: str,\n        rules: list[ExceptionRule],\n    ) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate exception rules against state changes.\n\n        Returns list of triggered rule outputs with project_slug, urgency, and exception type.\n        Rules fire only when matching state changes occur in the diff.\n        No firing when no relevant state changes.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not changes_dict.get(\"changed_fields\"):\n            return []\n\n        diff = changes_dict.get(\"diff\", {})\n        triggered_rules = []\n\n        for rule in rules:\n            # Check if rule applies to this project\n            if rule.project_slug and rule.project_slug != project_slug:\n                continue\n\n            # Evaluate rule condition against diff\n            if self._rule_condition_matches(rule.condition, diff):\n                triggered_rules.append({\n                    \"project_slug\": project_slug,\n                    \"urgency\": rule.urgency,\n                    \"exception_type\": rule.name,\n                    \"condition\": rule.condition,\n                    \"message\": rule.message,\n                })\n                logger.info(f\"Exception rule '{rule.name}' fired for {project_slug}: {rule.condition}\")\n\n        return triggered_rules\n\n    def _rule_condition_matches(self, condition: str, diff: dict) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a rule condition matches the diff.\n\n        Conditions are simple field comparisons against the diff.\n        Examples:\n        - \"phase==Failed\" -> true if phase changed to Failed\n        - \"restarts>5\" -> true if restarts changed to value > 5\n        - \"sync_status==OutOfSync\" -> true if sync_status changed to OutOfSync\n        \"\"\"\n        import re\n\n        # Parse condition\n        match = re.match(r'(\\w+)(==|!=|>=|<=|>|<)(.+)', condition)\n        if not match:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid rule condition: {condition}\")\n            return False\n\n        field, op, expected_value = match.groups()\n\n        # Check if this field changed\n        if field not in diff:\n            return False\n\n        # Get the new value (what it changed \"to\")\n        new_value = diff[field].get(\"to\")\n\n        if new_value is None:\n            return False\n\n        # Evaluate condition\n        try:\n            if op == \"==\":\n                return str(new_value) == expected_value\n            elif op == \"!=\":\n                return str(new_value) != expected_value\n            elif op == \">\":\n                return float(new_value) > float(expected_value)\n            elif op == \"<\":\n                return float(new_value) < float(expected_value)\n            elif op == \">=\":\n                return float(new_value) >= float(expected_value)\n            elif op == \"<=\":\n                return float(new_value) <= float(expected_value)\n        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n            logger.warning(f\"Rule condition evaluation failed: {condition} with value {new_value}\")\n            return False\n\n        return False\n\n    async def monitor_topic(self, rule: MonitoringRule) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Monitor a single topic on its check interval.\n\n        Runs in a loop until cancelled.\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Starting monitor for topic {rule.topic_id} (interval: {rule.check_interval}s)\")\n\n        # Use a default session for monitoring results\n        # In full implementation, this would use the active session\n        session_id = \"monitoring\"  # Placeholder\n\n        while self.running:\n            try:\n                # Check current state (uses      has_change, changes_dict = await self.detect_state_change(rule, current_state)\n\n                    if has_change:\n                        logger.info(f\"State change detected for {rule.topic_id}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                await asyncio.sleep(rule.check_interval)\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start ambient monitoring.\"\"\"\n        logger.info(\"Starting ambient monitoring service\")\n\n        # Load config\n        config = await self.load_config()\n\n        self.running = True\n\n        # Start a monitor task for each active topic\n        for rule in config.active_topics:\n            task = asyncio.create_task(self.monitor_topic(rule))\n            self.tasks.append(task)\n\n        # Start the ticker task for (via mtime) and reloads if needed.\n        Runs on the tick_interval_seconds from the config.\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(\"Config ticker started\")\n\n        while self.running:\n self._config_loader.get_config()\n\n                # Create a simple hash of the active topics to detect structural changes\n                import json\n                current_hash = hash(json.dumps(current_config.get(\"monitoring\", {}).get(\"active_topics\", []), sort_keys=True))\n\n                if self._last_tick_config_hash != current_hash:\n                    logger.info(\"Config structure changed, reloading monitors\")\n                    await self.reload_config()\n                    self._last_tick_config_hash = current_hash\n                else:\n                    logger.debug(\"Config structure unchanged, no reload needed\")\n\n                # Wait for next tick\n                await asyncio.sleep(tick_interval)\n\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in config ticker: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                await ambient monitoring service\")\n        self.running = False\n\n        # Cancel all monitor tasks\n        for task in self.tasks:\n            task.cancel()\n\n        # Wait for tasks to complete\n        await asyncio.gather(*self.tasks, return_exceptions=True)\n\n        self.tasks.clear()\n        self._ticker_task = None\n\n        # Close HTTP client\n        if self._http_client:\n            await self._http_client.close()\n\n        logger.info(\"Ambient monitoring stopped\")\n\n    async def reload_config(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Reload monitoring configuration and restart topic monitors.\"\"\"\n        logger.info(\"Reloading monitoring configuration\")\n\n        # Separate ticker task from monitor tasks\n        monitor_tasks = [t for t in self.tasks if t != {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_b9d67973b75140f2b4e12930","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tSSE broadcaster: stream results to connected surfaces.\n3\t\n4\tManages SSE connections and broadcasts events to relevant surfaces.\n5\t\"\"\"\n6\t\n7\timport asyncio\n8\timport json\n9\timport logging\n10\timport os\n11\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\n12\tfrom datetime import datetime\n13\tfrom typing import Dict\n14\tfrom uuid import uuid4\n15\t\n16\t# Idle-stream keepalive. An SSE connection that sends nothing while it waits for\n17\t# events is indistinguishable from a dead one: the browser's EventSource only\n18\t# fires ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail, so a *silent* network drop on an\n19\t# idle stream leaves the surface showing \"Connected\" indefinitely (until the\n20\t# OS-level TCP keepalive times out, many minutes later). Emitting a comment\n21\t# line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``) every few seconds keeps traffic flowing, so:\n22\t#\n23\t#   - proxies/load balancers won't reap the connection as idle, and\n24\t#   - the next ping after a real network drop fails to arrive, the client read\n25\t#     errors, ``onerror`` fires, and the native EventSource reconnects — which\n26\t#     is exactly the resync path the canvas relies on.\n27\t#\n28\t# Comment lines are ignored by EventSource (they never surface as messages), so\n29\t# this adds no client-visible events. ``5s`` is frequent enough that a drop is\n30\t# surfaced well inside any realistic reconnect budget, and quiet enough not to\n31\t# be chatty. Lower it (e.g. via ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS) for faster\n32\t# reconnection tests.\n33\tKEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = float(\n34\t    os.environ.get(\"ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS\", \"5\")\n35\t)\n36\t\n37\t# Sentinel pushed onto a connection's queue by :meth:`SSEBroadcaster.drop_session`\n38\t# to make that connection's :meth:`event_generator` return ABRUPTLY — i.e. without\n39\t# emitting a ``disconnect`` event. An abrupt end of the response body is what the\n40\t# browser's ``EventSource`` treats as a dropped connection: it fires ``onerror``\n41\t# and performs its NATIVE auto-reconnect. (The graceful ``disconnect`` event, by\n42\t# contrast, makes the client call ``close()`` and stay down.) This is the only\n43\t# way to faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop from a test\n44\t# against a loopback server — Playwright ``context.set_offline`` cannot break an\n45\t# already-established loopback SSE stream (see tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n46\t_DROP = object()\n47\t\n48\t\n49\tlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n50\t\n51\t\n52\t@dataclass\n53\tclass SSEConnection:\n54\t    \"\"\"An active SSE connection.\"\"\"\n55\t    connection_id: str\n56\t    surface_id: str\n57\t    session_id: str\n58\t    surface_type: str\n59\t    queue: asyncio.Queue = field(default_factory=asyncio.Queue)\n60\t    connected_at: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n61\t    last_heartbeat: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n62\t\n63\t\n64\t@dataclass\n65\tclass SSEEvent:\n66\t    \"\"\"An SSE event to broadcast.\n67\t\n68\t    Attributes:\n69\t        event_type: The type of SSE event (e.g., 'result_created', 'topic_updated')\n70\t        data: The event payload data\n71\t        rendered_html: Optional rendered HTML for canvas injection (e.g., pre-rendered card)\n72\t        target_session_id: Optional filter to only send to connections for this session\n73\t        target_surface_id: Optional filter to only send to this specific surface\n74\t        exclude_surface_id: Optional filter to exclude this surface from receiving the event\n75\t    \"\"\"\n76\t    event_type: str\n77\t    data: dict\n78\t    rendered_html: str | None = None\n79\t    target_session_id: str | None = None\n80\t    target_surface_id: str | None = None\n81\t    exclude_surface_id: str | None = None\n82\t\n83\t\n84\tclass SSEBroadcaster:\n85\t    \"\"\"\n86\t    Manages SSE connections and broadcasts events.\n87\t\n88\t    Events are queued per-connection and sent via SSE.\n89\t    \"\"\"\n90\t\n91\t    def __init__(self):\n92\t        self.connections: Dict[str, SSEConnection] = {}\n93\t        self._cleanup_task: asyncio.Task | None = None\n94\t        self._running = False\n95\t\n96\t    async def start(self) -> None:\n97\t        \"\"\"Start the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n98\t        self._running = True\n99\t        self._cleanup_task = asyncio.create_task(self._cleanup_loop())\n100\t\n101\t    async def stop(self) -> None:\n102\t        \"\"\"Stop the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n103\t        self._running = False\n104\t        if self._cleanup_task:\n105\t            self._cleanup_task.cancel()\n106\t            try:\n107\t                await self._cleanup_task\n108\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n109\t                pass\n110\t\n111\t    def register(\n112\t        self,\n113\t        surface_id: str,\n114\t        session_id: str,\n115\t        surface_type: str,\n116\t    ) -> SSEConnection:\n117\t        \"\"\"Register a new SSE connection.\"\"\"\n118\t        connection_id = str(uuid4())\n119\t        connection = SSEConnection(\n120\t            connection_id=connection_id,\n121\t            surface_id=surface_id,\n122\t            session_id=session_id,\n123\t            surface_type=surface_type,\n124\t        )\n125\t        self.connections[connection_id] = connection\n126\t        logger.info(f\"Registered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {surface_id}\")\n127\t        return connection\n128\t\n129\t    def unregister(self, connection_id: str) -> None:\n130\t        \"\"\"Unregister an SSE connection.\"\"\"\n131\t        if connection_id in self.connections:\n132\t            conn = self.connections[connection_id]\n133\t            logger.info(f\"Unregistered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {conn.surface_id}\")\n134\t            del self.connections[connection_id]\n135\t\n136\t    def drop_session(self, session_id: str) -> int:\n137\t        \"\"\"Abruptly drop every live SSE stream for ``session_id``.\n138\t\n139\t        Pushes the ``_DROP`` sentinel onto each matching connection's queue so\n140\t        its :meth:`event_generator` returns without emitting a ``disconnect``\n141\t        event — the browser's ``EventSource`` then sees the stream end abruptly,\n142\t        fires ``onerror``, and performs its native auto-reconnect. Returns the\n143\t        number of streams signalled. Used by ``POST /api/v1/test/drop-sse`` to\n144\t        faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop, which\n145\t        ``context.set_offline`` cannot reproduce against a loopback server\n146\t        (loopback connections are exempt from offline emulation — see\n147\t        tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n148\t        \"\"\"\n149\t        count = 0\n150\t        for conn in self.connections.values():\n151\t            if conn.session_id == session_id:\n152\t                conn.queue.put_nowait(_DROP)\n153\t                count += 1\n154\t        if count:\n155\t            logger.info(f\"Drop requested for session {session_id}: {count} stream(s)\")\n156\t        return count\n157\t\n158\t    def heartbeat(self, connection_id: str) -> bool:\n159\t        \"\"\"Update heartbeat for a connection. Returns True if connection exists.\"\"\"\n160\t        if connection_id in self.connections:\n161\t            self.connections[connection_id].last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n162\t            return True\n163\t        return False\n164\t\n165\t    async def broadcast(self, event: SSEEvent) -> int:\n166\t        \"\"\"\n167\t        Broadcast an event to relevant connections.\n168\t\n169\t        Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n170\t        \"\"\"\n171\t        sent_count = 0\n172\t\n173\t        for conn in list(self.connections.values()):\n174\t            # Filter by target\n175\t            if event.target_session_id and conn.session_id != event.target_session_id:\n176\t                continue\n177\t            if event.target_surface_id and conn.surface_id != event.target_surface_id:\n178\t                continue\n179\t            if event.exclude_surface_id and conn.surface_id == event.exclude_surface_id:\n180\t                continue\n181\t\n182\t            # Queue the event\n183\t            try:\n184\t                conn.queue.put_nowait(event)\n185\t                sent_count += 1\n186\t            except asyncio.QueueFull:\n187\t                logger.warning(f\"Queue full for connection {conn.connection_id}, dropping event\")\n188\t\n189\t        return sent_count\n190\t\n191\t    async def event_generator(self, connection: SSEConnection):\n192\t        \"\"\"\n193\t        Generate SSE events for a connection.\n194\t\n195\t        Yields formatted SSE messages.\n196\t        \"\"\"\n197\t        try:\n198\t            # Send initial connection event\n199\t            yield self._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n200\t                \"connection_id\": connection.connection_id,\n201\t                \"surface_id\": connection.surface_id,\n202\t                \"session_id\": connection.session_id,\n203\t            })\n204\t\n205\t            while True:\n206\t                # Block on the next event, but no longer than the keepalive\n207\t                # interval: if nothing arrives, emit a comment-line ping so the\n208\t                # stream is never silent. A silent idle stream is indistinguish-\n209\t                # able from a dead one to the browser — EventSource only fires\n210\t                # ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail — so without these pings\n211\t                # a real network drop on an idle surface leaves it showing\n212\t                # \"Connected\" until the OS TCP keepalive times out (minutes\n213\t                # later). The ping is a comment line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``), which\n214\t                # EventSource ignores — it surfaces no message to the client.\n215\t                try:\n216\t                    event = await asyncio.wait_for(\n217\t                        connection.queue.get(),\n218\t                        timeout=KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS,\n219\t                    )\n220\t                except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n221\t                    yield \": ping\\n\\n\"\n222\t                    continue\n223\t\n224\t                # Abrupt drop sentinel (see _DROP): end the stream WITHOUT a\n225\t                # disconnect event so the browser EventSource onerrors + reconnects.\n226\t                if event is _DROP:\n227\t                    logger.info(f\"Drop sentinel on connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n228\t                    return\n229\t\n230\t                connection.last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n231\t\n232\t                # Format and yield the event\n233\t                payload = dict(event.data)\n234\t                if event.rendered_html is not None:\n235\t                    payload[\"rendered_html\"] = event.rendered_html\n236\t                yield self._format_sse(event.event_type, payload)\n237\t\n238\t                # Special case: disconnect event ends the stream\n239\t                if event.event_type == \"disconnect\":\n240\t                    logger.info(f\"Disconnect event for connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n241\t                    break\n242\t\n243\t        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n244\t            logger.info(f\"Connection {connection.connection_id} cancelled\")\n245\t            raise\n246\t        finally:\n247\t            self.unregister(connection.connection_id)\n248\t\n249\t    def _format_sse(self, event_type: str, data: dict) -> str:\n250\t        \"\"\"Format an event as SSE.\"\"\"\n251\t        return f\"event: {event_type}\\ndata: {json.dumps(data)}\\n\\n\"\n252\t\n253\t    async def _cleanup_loop(self):\n254\t        \"\"\"Periodically clean up dead connections.\"\"\"\n255\t        while self._running:\n256\t            try:\n257\t                await asyncio.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds\n258\t\n259\t                now = datetime.now().timestamp()\n260\t                timeout = 300  # 5 minutes\n261\t\n262\t                dead_connections = [\n263\t                    cid for cid, conn in self.connections.items()\n264\t                    if (now - conn.last_heartbeat) > timeout\n265\t                ]\n266\t\n267\t                for cid in dead_connections:\n268\t                    logger.info(f\"Cleaning up dead connection {cid}\")\n269\t                    # Send disconnect event before removing\n270\t                    try:\n271\t                        conn = self.connections[cid]\n272\t                        conn.queue.put_nowait(SSEEvent(\n273\t                            event_type=\"disconnect\",\n274\t                            data={\"reason\": \"timeout\"},\n275\t                        ))\n276\t                    except asyncio.QueueFull:\n277\t                        pass\n278\t                    self.unregister(cid)\n279\t\n280\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n281\t                break\n282\t            except Exception as e:\n283\t                logger.error(f\"Error in cleanup loop: {e}\")\n284\t\n285\t\n286\t# Global broadcaster instance\n287\t_broadcaster: \"intent_resolved\"\n318\t\n319\t    # Topic events\n320\t    TOPIC_CREATED = \"topic_created\"\n321\t    TOPIC_UPDATED = \"topic_updated\"\n322\t    TOPIC_STALE = \"topic_stale\"\n323\t\n324\t    # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n284\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n285\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n286\t\n287\t            # Create schema\n288\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n289\t            await db.commit()\n290\t\n291\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n292\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n293\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n294\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n295\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n296\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n297\t\n298\t    @staticmethod\n299\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n300\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n301\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n302\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n303\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n304\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n305\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n306\t            await db.execute(\n307\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n308\t            )\n309\t\n310\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n311\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n312\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n313\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n314\t\n315\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n316\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n317\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n318\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n319\t\n320\t        # Migrate results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\n321\t        await SessionStore._migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db)\n322\t\n323\t        # Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\n324\t        await SessionStore._migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db)\n325\t\n326\t        await db.commit()\n327\t\n328\t    @staticmethod\n329\t    async def _migrate_intents_status_enum(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n330\t        \"\"\"Migrate intents table to support 'stuck' and 'failed' status values.\n331\t\n332\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: extend intents status enum with stuck/failed.\n333\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.\n334\t        \"\"\"\n335\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n336\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n337\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n338\t            status_col = None\n339\t            for col in cols:\n340\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n341\t                    status_col = col\n342\t                    break\n343\t\n344\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n345\t        if not status_col:\n346\t            return\n347\t\n348\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n349\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n350\t        async with db.execute(\n351\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n352\t        ) as cur:\n353\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n354\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n355\t                # Copy data from old table to new table\n386\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n387\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n388\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n389\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n390\t                FROM intents\n391\t            \"\"\")\n392\t\n393\t            # Build update dynamically based on which values are provided\n827\t            updates = []\n828\t            params = []\n829\t\n830\t            if intent_type is not None:\n831\t                updates.append(\"intent_type = ?\")\n832\t                params.append(intent_type)\n833\t\n834\t            if status is not None:\n835\t                updates.append(\"status = ?\")\n836\t                params.append(status)\n837\t                # Nothing to update\n847\t                return\n848\t\n849\t            params.append(intent_id)\n850\t            query = f\"UPDATE intents SET {', '.join(updates)} WHERE id = ?\"\n851\t            await db.execute(query, params)\n852\t            await db.commit()\n853\t\n854\t    async def update_intent_topic(\n855\t        self,\n856\t        intent_id: str,\n857\t        topic_id: str | None,\n858\t    ) -> None:\n859\t        \"\"\"Update intent's primary topic.\n860\t\n861\t        Args:\n862\t            intent_id: The intent ID to update\n863\t            topic_id: New topic ID (None = don't update)\n864\t        \"\"\"\n865\t        if topic_id is None:\n866\t            return\n867\t\n868\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n869\t            await db.execute(\n870\t                \"UPDATE intents SET topic_id = ? Ensure the row exists (created_at set once, on first write).\n1507\t            await db.execute(\n1508\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dispatch_timings (intent_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n1509\t                (intent_id, now),\n1510\t            )\n1511\t            if cols:\n1512\t                set_clause = \", \".join(f\"{c} = ?\" for c in cols)\n1513\t                params = [*cols.values(), intent_id]\n1514\t                await db.execute(\n1515\t                    f\"UPDATE dispatch_timings SET {set_clause} WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1516\t                    params,\n1517\t                )\n1518\t            await db.commit()\n1519\t\n1520\t    async def get_dispatch_timings(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n1521\t        \"\"\"Get the dispatch_timings row for one intent thread, or None.\"\"\"\n1522\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1523\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1524\t            async with db.execute(\n1525\t                \"SELECT * FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1526\t                (intent_id,),\n1527\t            ) as cursor:\n1528\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n1529\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n1530\t\n1531\t    async def get_latency_percentiles(self, since: int | None = None) -> dict:\n1532\t        \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per stage across dispatch_timings rows.\n1533\t\n1534\t        Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n1535\t        that has at least one non-NULL value. NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n1536\t        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n1537\t        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n1538\t        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n1539\t        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n1540\t\n1541\t        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n1542\t        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n1543\t        \"\"\"\n1544\t        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n1545\t\n1546\t        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n1547\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1548\t            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n1549\t                if since is not None:\n1550\t                    query = (\n1551\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1552\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? \"\n1553\t                        f\"ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1554\t                    )\n1555\t                    params: tuple = (since,)\n1556\t                else:\n1557\t                    query = (\n1558\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1559\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1560\t                    )\n1561\t                    params = ()\n1562\t                async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n1563\t                    values = [row[0] for row in await cursor.fetchall()]\n1564\t                if not values:\n1565\t                    continue\n1566\t                pct = percentiles(values, qs=(50, 95))\n1567\t                result[stage] = {\n1568\t                    \"p50\": pct[50],\n1569\t                    \"p95\": pct[95],\n1570\t                    \"count\": len(values),\n1571\t                }\n1572\t        return result\n1573\t\n1574\t    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n1575\t        self,\n1576\t        session_id: str,\n1577\t        since: int | None = None,\n1578\t    ) -> dict:\n1579\t        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n1580\t        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n1581\t\n1582\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1583\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1584\t\n1585\t            # Get signal counts by type\n1586\t            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n1587\t            async with db.execute(\n1588\t                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n1589\t                   FROM feedback_signals\n1590\t                   WHERE session_id = ? Overridable via ADC_DB_PATH env var so tests can point at an\n# isolated temp/in-memory DB instead of the production data/session.db.\nDEFAULT_DB_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/data/session.db\")\n\n# Schema definition\nSCHEMA_SQL = \"\"\"\n-- Sessions: surface-agnostic persistent entities\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (\n    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    last_active     INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    primary_surface_id TEXT\n);\n\n-- Surfaces: transient windows into sessions\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS surfaces (\n    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    session_id      TEXT NOT NULL,\n    type            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('canvas', 'telegram', 'audio')),\n    state           TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(state IN ('active', 'idle', 'disconnected')) DEFAULT 'active',\n    always_available INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(always_available IN (0, 1)),\n    last_seen       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_surfaces_session ON surfaces(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_surfaces_state ON surfaces(state);\n\n-- Utterances: raw input from user\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS utterances (\n    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    raw_text    TEXT NOT NULL,\n    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_utterances_session ON utterances(session_id);\n\n-- Intents: parsed intent threads from utterances\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intents (\n    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n    topic_id     TEXT,\n    project_slug TEXT,\n    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    lookup_kind  TEXT,  -- lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs' (router-emitted; see Intent Router). The hot-path\n                       -- component lookup keys on this column, no LLM (see UI-Regen Agent /\n                       -- component_usage_patterns).\n    card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),  -- 1 when no component matched this result_type (or below threshold): the client renders the built-in generic fallback card (see Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card). 0 when a real component rendered it (card_cache holds the rendered HTML).\n    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n    acked_at    INTEGER,\n    previous_result_id TEXT,  -- Link to previous result for diff\n    diff_summary TEXT,  -- Human-readable diff summary\n    diff_data    TEXT,  -- JSON: detailed field diffs\n    FOREIGN KEY FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_session ON results(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_topic ON results(topic_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_created ON results(created_at);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_previous ON results(previous_result_id);\n\n-- Topics: persistent concerns organizing intents and results\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topics (\n    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    label        TEXT NOT NULL,\n    type         TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')) DEFAULT 'adhoc',\n    project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n    scope        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(scope IN ('session', 'cross-session', 'global')) DEFAULT 'session',\n    session_id   TEXT,\n    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    last_active  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    archived_at  INTEGER,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_session ON topics(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_scope ON topics(scope);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS  context_data TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: pre-fetched context (kubectl, git, beads results)\n    fetched_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    expires_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES intent_topics (\n    intent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n    topic_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\n-- Feedback signals: implicit user behavior tracking for background analysis\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS feedback_signals (\n    signal_id sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT
