KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n142\t);\n143\t\n144\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_context_expires ON topic_context_cache(expires_at);\n145\t\n146\t-- Intent-topic many-to-many relationship\n147\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intent_topics (\n148\t    intent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n149\t    topic_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n150\t    PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id),\n151\t    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n152\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n153\t);\n154\t\n155\t-- Feedback signals: implicit user behavior tracking for background analysis\n156\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS feedback_signals (\n157\t    signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n158\t    signal_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n159\t    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n160\t    result_id    TEXT,\n161\t    topic_id     TEXT,\n162\t    timestamp    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n163\t    data         TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n164\t    surface_type TEXT,\n165\t    processed    INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(processed IN (0, 1)),\n166\t    processed_at INTEGER,\n167\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n168\t    FOREIGN KEY (result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n169\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n170\t);\n171\t\n172\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_session ON feedback_signals(session_id);\n173\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON feedback_signals(signal_type);\n174\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_processed ON feedback_signals(processed);\n175\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n176\t\n177\t-- Per-stage latency capture for every dispatch (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n178\t-- Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n179\t-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n180\t-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n181\t-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n182\t-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n183\t-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n184\t-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n185\t-- first token separately) — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n186\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dispatch_timings (\n187\t    intent_id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n188\t    router_ms                 INTEGER,\n189\t    fetch_first_source_ms     INTEGER,\n190\t    fetch_total_ms            INTEGER,\n191\t    synthesize_first_token_ms INTEGER,\n192\t    synthesize_total_ms       INTEGER,\n193\t    escalate_ms               INTEGER,\n194\t    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n195\t    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n196\t    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n197\t    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n198\t);\n199\t\n200\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n201\t\n202\t-- Bead watch table: circuit breaker tracking for async beads\n203\t-- Persistence layer for the async-path circuit breaker (plan §10 The Async Path).\n204\t-- Tracks open beads watched by the BeadWatcher, recording refusal counts,\n205\t-- last refusal reasons, SLA deadlines, and fencing state. All state is persisted\n206\t-- so a watcher restart loses nothing -- breaker state lives in the database,\n207\t-- not only in memory.\n208\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_watch (\n209\t    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- References bead ID (intents.bead_ref)\n210\t    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of REFUSED: comments seen\n211\t    last_refusal_reason TEXT,  -- Most recent refusal reason\n212\t    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,  -- Timestamp of most recent refusal\n213\t    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,  -- Latest comment index processed (-1 = none)\n214\t    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Unix timestamp when SLA expires\n215\t    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when SLA was flagged (NULL if not flagged)\n216\t    fenced_at          INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when bead was fenced to status=blocked (NULL if not fenced)\n217\t    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL  -- When this watch row was created\n218\t);\n219\t\n220\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch(sla_deadline);\n221\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch(fenced_at);\n222\t\n223\t-- Card cache: pre-rendered HTML for result components\n224\t-- Stores server-side rendered HTML for result/component/layout combinations.\n225\t-- Primary key (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket) allows multiple cached\n226\t-- variations per result (e.g., different layouts). Populated after component\n227\t-- selection to avoid repeated rendering for the same result.\n228\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS card_cache (\n229\t    result_id      TEXT NOT NULL,\n230\t    component_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n231\t    layout_bucket  TEXT NOT NULL,\n232\t    rendered_html  TEXT NOT NULL,\n233\t    created_at     INTEGER NOT NULL,\n234\t    PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n235\t);\n236\t\n237\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_card_cache_result_id ON card_cache(result_id);\n238\t\"\"\"\n239\t\n240\t# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n241\t# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\n242\tDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n243\t    \"router_ms\",\n244\t    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n245\t    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n246\t    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n247\t    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n248\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n249\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n250\t    \"stt_ms\",\n251\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n252\t)\n253\t\n254\t# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The Async Path: stuck/failed added for circuit breaker)\n255\tINTENT_STATUSES = ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')\n256\t\n257\t# Default SLA hours per intent type (plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n258\t# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n259\t# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\n260\tDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, float] = {\n261\t    \"task-profile\": 6.0,  # 6 hours for async bead-backed tasks\n262\t    \"status\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path status intents\n263\t    \"action\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path action intents\n264\t    \"lookup\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path lookup intents\n265\t    \"brainstorm\": 0.5,   # 30 minutes for brainstorm (may need user iteration)\n266\t    \"reminder\": 24.0,    # 24 hours for reminders\n267\t}\n268\t\n269\t# Circuit breaker thresholds (plan §10 The Async Path)\n270\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD = 3  # Fence after N refusals\n271\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 24.0  # Fence after N hours without progress\n272\t\n273\t\n274\tclass SessionStore:\n275\t    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n276\t\n277\t    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n278\t        self.db_path = db_path\n279\t\n280\t    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n281\t        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n282\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n283\t            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n284\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n285\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n286\t\n287\t            # Create schema\n288\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n289\t            await db.commit()\n290\t\n291\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n292\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n293\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n294\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n295\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n296\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n297\t\n298\t    @staticmethod\n299\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n300\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n301\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n302\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n303\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n304\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n305\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n306\t            await db.execute(\n307\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n308\t            )\n309\t\n310\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n311\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n312\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n313\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n314\t\n315\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n316\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n317\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n318\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n319\t\n320\t        # Migrate results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\n321\t        await SessionStore._migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db)\n322\t\n323\t        # Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\n324\t        await SessionStore._migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db)\n325\t\n326\t        await db.commit()\n327\t\n328\t    @staticmethod\n329\t    async def _migrate_intents_status_enum(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n330\t        \"\"\"Migrate intents table to support 'stuck' and 'failed' status values.\n331\t\n332\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: extend intents status enum with stuck/failed.\n333\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.\n334\t        \"\"\"\n335\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n336\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n337\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n338\t            status_col = None\n339\t            for col in cols:\n340\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n341\t                    status_col = col\n342\t                    break\n343\t\n344\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n345\t        if not status_col:\n346\t            return\n347\t\n348\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n349\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n350\t        async with db.execute(\n351\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n352\t        ) as cur:\n353\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n354\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n355\t                # Already migrated\n356\t                return\n357\t\n358\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n359\t        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n360\t\n361\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n362\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n363\t\n364\t        try:\n365\t            # Create new table with updated constraint\n366\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n367\t                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n368\t                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n369\t                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n370\t                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n371\t                    topic_id     TEXT,\n372\t                    project_slug TEXT,\n373\t                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n374\t                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n375\t                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n376\t                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n377\t                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n378\t                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n379\t                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n380\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n381\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n382\t                )\n383\t            \"\"\")\n384\t\n385\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n386\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n387\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n388\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n389\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n390\t                FROM intents\n391\t            \"\"\")\n392\t\n393\t            # Recreate indexes\n394\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_session\")\n395\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_topic\")\n396\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_status\")\n397\t\n398\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_session ON intents_new(session_id)\")\n399\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_topic ON intents_new(topic_id)\")\n400\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_status ON intents_new(status)\")\n401\t\n402\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n403\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE intents\")\n404\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents_new RENAME TO intents\")\n405\t\n406\t            await db.commit()\n407\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: intents.status now supports 'stuck' and 'failed'\")\n408\t\n409\t        except Exception as e:\n410\t            await db.rollback()\n411\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate intents.status: {e}\")\n412\t            raise\n413\t\n414\t    @staticmethod\n415\t    async def _migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n416\t        \"\"\"Migrate results table to allow NULL intent_id for monitoring-originated results.\n417\t\n418\t        Plan §10 Bead Watcher: monitoring-originated results have intent_id=NULL.\n419\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN to drop NOT NULL, so we recreate the table.\n420\t        \"\"\"\n421\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the results table schema\n422\t        async with db.execute(\n423\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='results'\"\n424\t        ) as cur:\n425\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n426\t            if not table_sql:\n427\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n428\t\n429\t            # If intent_id is already nullable (NOT NULL not present), skip migration\n430\t            if \"intent_id   TEXT,\" in table_sql[0] or \"intent_id TEXT,\" in table_sql[0]:\n431\t                if \"NOT NULL\" not in table_sql[0].split(\"intent_id\")[1].split(\",\")[0]:\n432\t                    # Already migrated\n433\t                    return\n434\t\n435\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with nullable intent_id\n436\t        logger.info(\"Migrating results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\")\n437\t\n438\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n439\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n440\t\n441\t        try:\n442\t            # Create new table with nullable intent_id\n443\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n444\t                CREATE TABLE results_new (\n445\t                    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n446\t                    intent_id   TEXT,\n447\t                    topic_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n448\t                    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n449\t                    summary     TEXT NOT NULL,\n450\t                    data        TEXT NOT NULL,\n451\t                    urgency     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(urgency IN ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')) DEFAULT 'normal',\n452\t                    result_type TEXT,\n453\t                    card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),\n454\t                    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n455\t                    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n456\t                    acked_at    INTEGER,\n457\t                    previous_result_id TEXT,\n458\t                    diff_summary TEXT,\n459\t                    diff_data    TEXT,\n460\t                    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n461\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n462\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n463\t                    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n464\t                )\n465\t            \"\"\")\n466\t\n467\t            # Copy data from old table to new table - explicit column mapping\n468\t            # to handle different column orders between old and new schemas\n469\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n470\t                INSERT INTO results_new (id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency, result_type, card_fallback, created_at, surfaced_at, acked_at, previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data)\n471\t                SELECT id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency,\n472\t                       COALESCE(result_type, 'default') AS result_type,\n473\t                       COALESCE(card_fallback, 0) AS card_fallback,\n474\t                       created_at, surfaced_at, acked_at, previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data\n475\t                FROM results\n476\t            \"\"\")\n477\t\n478\t            # Recreate indexes\n479\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_session\")\n480\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_topic\")\n481\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_created\")\n482\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_previous\")\n483\t\n484\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_session ON results_new(session_id)\")\n485\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_topic ON results_new(topic_id)\")\n486\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_created ON results_new(created_at)\")\n487\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_previous ON results_new(previous_result_id)\")\n488\t\n489\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n490\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE results\")\n491\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results_new RENAME TO results\")\n492\t\n493\t            await db.commit()\n494\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: results.intent_id now allows NULL\")\n495\t        except Exception as e:\n496\t            await db.rollback()\n497\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate results.intent_id: {e}\")\n498\t            raise\n499\t\n500\t    @staticmethod\n501\t    async def _migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n502\t        \"\"\"Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1.\n503\t\n504\t        The initial schema had DEFAULT 0, which meant the first comment (index 0)\n505\t        was skipped on the first check. With DEFAULT -1, no comments are skipped\n506\t        initially, and only comments with index > comment_high_water are processed.\n507\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN DEFAULT, so we recreate the table.\n508\t        \"\"\"\n509\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the bead_watch table schema\n510\t        async with db.execute(\n511\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='bead_watch'\"\n512\t        ) as cur:\n513\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n514\t            if not table_sql:\n515\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n516\t\n517\t            # If comment_high_water already has DEFAULT -1, skip migration\n518\t            if \"DEFAULT -1\" in table_sql[0]:\n519\t                # Already migrated\n520\t                return\n521\t\n522\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new default\n523\t        logger.info(\"Migrating bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\")\n524\t\n525\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n526\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n527\t\n528\t        try:\n529\t            # Create new table with DEFAULT -1\n530\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n531\t                CREATE TABLE bead_watch_new (\n532\t                    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n533\t                    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n534\t                    last_refusal_reason TEXT,\n535\t                    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,\n536\t                    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,\n537\t                    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n538\t                    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,\n539\t                    fenced_at          INTEGER,\n540\t                    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL\n541\t                )\n542\t            \"\"\")\n543\t\n544\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n545\t            # Existing rows with comment_high_water=0 are migrated as-is\n546\t            # (they represent \"comment 0 has been processed\", which is valid)\n547\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n548\t                INSERT INTO bead_watch_new\n549\t                SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n550\t            \"\"\")\n551\t\n552\t            # Recreate indexes\n553\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline\")\n554\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced\")\n555\t\n556\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch_new(sla_deadline)\")\n557\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch_new(fenced_at)\")\n558\t\n559\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n560\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE bead_watch\")\n561\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE bead_watch_new RENAME TO bead_watch\")\n562\t\n563\t            await db.commit()\n564\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: bead_watch.comment_high_water default is now -1\")\n565\t        except Exception as e:\n566\t            await db.rollback()\n567\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water: {e}\")\n568\t            raise\n569\t\n570\t    async def close(self) -> None:\n571\t        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n572\t        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n573\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n574\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n575\t\n576\t    # Session operations\n577\t    async def create_session(self, session_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n578\t        \"\"\"Create a new session and return its ID.\"\"\"\n579\t        if not session_id:\n580\t            session_id = str(uuid4())\n581\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n582\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n583\t            await db.execute(\n584\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO sessions (id, created_at, last_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n585\t                (session_id, now, now)\n586\t            )\n587\t            await db.commit()\n588\t        return session_id\n589\t\n590\t    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n591\t        \"\"\"Get session by ID.\"\"\"\n592\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n593\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n594\t            async with db.execute(\n595\t                \"SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n596\t            ) as cursor:\n597\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n598\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n599\t\n600\t    async def update_session_activity(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n601\t        \"\"\"Update session last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n602\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n603\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n604\t            await db.execute(\n605\t                \"UPDATE sessions SET last_active = ? Note: SQLite FK ``ON DELETE CASCADE`` is *not* enforced\n625\t        here (``PRAGMA foreign_keys`` is never enabled), so child rows are\n626\t        deleted explicitly, in dependency order, within one transaction.\n627\t\n628\t        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n629\t        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n630\t        explicitly is required for a true clean teardown.\n631\t\n632\t        Returns ``{\"session_removed\": 0|1, \"topics_removed\": <int>}``.\n633\t        \"\"\"\n634\t        async with cur.fetchone())[0]\n642\t            async with db.execute(\n643\t                \"SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n644\t            ) as cur:\n645\t                session_removed = 1 if await cur.fetchone() else 0\n646\t\n647\t            # Children first, parents last (matches FK dependency order).\n648\t            await db.execute(\n649\t                \"DELETE FROM feedback_signals WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n650\t            )\n651\t            await db.execute(\n652\t                \"DELETE FROM results WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n653\t            )\n654\t            await db.execute(\n655\t                \"DELETE FROM intent_topics WHERE intent_id IN \"\n656\t                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE \"DELETE FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n671\t            )\n672\t            await db.execute(\n673\t                \"DELETE FROM utterances WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n674\t            )\n675\t            await db.execute(\n676\t                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id IN \"\n677\t                \"(SELECT id FROM topics WHERE           )\n683\t            await db.execute(\n684\t                \"DELETE FROM surfaces WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n685\t            )\n686\t            await db.execute(\n687\t                \"DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n688\t            )\n689\t            await db.commit()\n690\t\n691\t        return {\"session_removed\": session_removed, Ensure the row exists (created_at set once, on first write).\n1507\t            await db.execute(\n1508\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dispatch_timings (intent_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n1509\t                (intent_id, now),\n1510\t            )\n1511\t            if cols:\n1512\t                set_clause = \", \".join(f\"{c} = ?\" for c in cols)\n1513\t                params = [*cols.values(), intent_id]\n1514\t                await db.execute(\n1515\t                    f\"UPDATE dispatch_timings SET {set_clause} WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1516\t                    params,\n1517\t                )\n1518\t            await db.commit()\n1519\t\n1520\t    async def get_dispatch_timings(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n1521\t        \"\"\"Get the dispatch_timings row for one intent thread, or None.\"\"\"\n1522\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1523\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1524\t            async with db.execute(\n1525\t                \"SELECT * FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1526\t                (intent_id,),\n1527\t            ) as cursor:\n1528\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n1529\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n1530\t\n1531\t    async def get_latency_percentiles(self, since: int | None = None) -> dict:\n1532\t        \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per stage across dispatch_timings rows.\n1533\t\n1534\t        Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n1535\t        that has at least one non-NULL value. NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n1536\t        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n1537\t        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n1538\t        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n1539\t        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n1540\t\n1541\t        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n1542\t        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n1543\t        \"\"\"\n1544\t        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n1545\t\n1546\t        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n1547\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1548\t            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n1549\t                if since is not None:\n1550\t                    query = (\n1551\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1552\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? \"\n1553\t                        f\"ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1554\t                    )\n1555\t                    params: tuple = (since,)\n1556\t                else:\n1557\t                    query = (\n1558\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1559\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1560\t                    )\n1561\t                    params = ()\n1562\t                async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n1563\t                    values = [row[0] for row in await cursor.fetchall()]\n1564\t                if not values:\n1565\t                    continue\n1566\t                pct = percentiles(values, qs=(50, 95))\n1567\t                result[stage] = {\n1568\t                    \"p50\": pct[50],\n1569\t                    \"p95\": pct[95],\n1570\t                    \"count\": len(values),\n1571\t                }\n1572\t        return result\n1573\t\n1574\t    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n1575\t        self,\n1576\t        session_id: str,\n1577\t        since: int | None = None,\n1578\t    ) -> dict:\n1579\t        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n1580\t        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n1581\t\n1582\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1583\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1584\t\n1585\t            # Get signal counts by type\n1586\t            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n1587\t            async with db.execute(\n1588\t                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n1589\t                   FROM feedback_signals\n1590\t                   WHERE session_id = ? It NEVER writes component definitions\n16\t(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\n17\tUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n18\t\"\"\"\n19\t\n20\timport html\n21\timport json\n22\timport re\n23\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n24\tfrom logging import getLogger\n25\tfrom typing import Any, Optional\n26\t\n27\tfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n28\t\n29\tlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n30\t\n31\t# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n32\t# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\n33\tDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n34\t\n35\t# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\n36\tDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n37\t\n38\t# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n39\t# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n40\t_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n41\t\n42\t\n43\tdef derive_result_type(\n44\t    intent_type: Optional[str],\n45\t    project_slug: Optional[str],\n46\t    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n47\t) -> str:\n48\t    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n49\t\n50\t    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n51\t      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n52\t    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n53\t      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n54\t      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n55\t    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n56\t\n57\t    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n58\t    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n59\t    \"\"\"\n60\t    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n61\t    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n62\t    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n63\t        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n64\t    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n65\t        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n66\t    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n67\t\n68\t\n69\t@dataclass\n70\tclass RenderOutcome:\n71\t    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n72\t\n73\t    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n74\t    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n75\t    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n76\t    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n77\t    \"\"\"\n78\t\n79\t    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n80\t    component_id: Optional[str]\n81\t    card_fallback: bool\n82\t    layout_bucket: str\n83\t\n84\t\n85\tclass HotPathRenderer:\n86\t    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n87\t\n88\t    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n89\t    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n90\t    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n91\t    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n92\t    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n93\t    \"\"\"\n94\t\n95\t    def __init__(\n96\t        self,\n97\t        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n98\t        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n99\t        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n100\t    ):\n101\t        self.library = library or get_library()\n102\t        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n103\t        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n104\t\n105\t    def render(\n106\t        self,\n107\t        result_id: str,\n108\t        result_type: str,\n109\t        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n110\t        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n111\t        urgency: Optional[str] = None,\n112\t        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n113\t    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n114\t        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n115\t\n116\t        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n117\t        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n118\t        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n119\t        outcome with the rendered fallback HTML and writes nothing to the\n120\t        component DB.\n121\t        \"\"\"\n122\t        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n123\t\n124\t        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n125\t            result_type, self.match_threshold\n126\t        )\n127\t        if component is None:\n128\t            logger.debug(\n129\t                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n130\t                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n131\t                result_type,\n132\t                self.match_threshold,\n133\t                result_id,\n134\t            )\n135\t            # Render the fallback card server-side for SSE streaming\n136\t            fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n137\t                summary=summary,\n138\t                data=result_data,\n139\t                urgency=urgency,\n140\t            )\n141\t            return RenderOutcome(\n142\t                rendered_html=fallback_html,\n143\t                component_id=None,\n144\t                card_fallback=True,\n145\t                layout_bucket=bucket,\n146\t            )\n147\t\n148\t        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n149\t\n150\t        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n151\t        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n152\t        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n153\t        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n154\t        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n155\t        self.library.cache_card(\n156\t            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n157\t        )\n158\t        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n159\t        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n160\t        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n161\t\n162\t        logger.info(\n163\t            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n164\t            result_id,\n165\t            component.id,\n166\t            component.version,\n167\t            result_type,\n168\t        )\n169\t        return RenderOutcome(\n170\t            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n171\t            component_id=component.id,\n172\t            card_fallback=False,\n173\t            layout_bucket=bucket,\n174\t        )\n175\t\n176\t\n177\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n178\t# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n179\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n180\t\n181\tdef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n182\t    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n183\t\n184\t    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n185\t    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n186\t    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n187\t    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n188\t    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n189\t    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n190\t    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n191\t    \"\"\"\n192\t    if not template:\n193\t        return \"\"\n194\t\n195\t    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n196\t        path = match.group(1).strip()\n197\t        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n198\t        if value is None:\n199\t            return \"\"\n200\t        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n201\t\n202\t    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n203\t\n204\t\n205\tdef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n238\t    if data is None:\n239\t        return []\n240\t\n241\t    obj = data\n242\t\n243\t    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n244\t    if isinstance(data, str):\n245\t _renderer\n431\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6316d8e8-ca7e-49b1-9f79-fc74f6fd735f","uuid":"806b81c9-75fa-4dc6-9f44-7af50473cc60","timestamp":"2026-07-23T20:23:29.274Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/render/hot_path.py","content":"\"\"\"Deterministic, no-LLM server-side card selector (the dispatch hot path).\n\nOn every dispatch the server picks the component to render a result\n*deterministically* — the highest ``match_score`` row in\n``component_usage_patterns`` for the result's ``result_type``, no LLM call —\nfills that component's template with the result data per layout bucket, writes\nthe rendered HTML to ``card_cache``, records the usage, and returns it for SSE\nstreaming. When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above\nthreshold) the result is *flagged* (``card_fallback``) and the client renders\nthe built-in generic fallback card — a novel shape never blanks the canvas.\n\nScope of writes (plan, Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card +\nUI-Regen Agent): the hot-path renderer is the sole writer of ``card_cache`` and\nthe component usage stats (``components.usage_count`` / ``last_used`` and\n``component_usage_patterns``). It NEVER writes component definitions\n(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\nUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport html\nimport json\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\nDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n\n# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\nDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n\n# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n\n\ndef derive_result_type(\n    intent_type: Optional[str],\n    project_slug: Optional[str],\n    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n\n    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n\n    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n    \"\"\"\n    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RenderOutcome:\n    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n\n    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n    component_id: Optional[str]\n    card_fallback: bool\n    layout_bucket: str\n\n\nclass HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n\n    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    ):\n        self.library = library or get_library()\n        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n\n    def render(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        result_type: str,\n        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n        urgency: Optional[str] = None,\n        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n\n        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n        outcome with the rendered fallback HTML and writes nothing to the\n        component DB.\n        \"\"\"\n        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n\n        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n            result_type, self.match_threshold\n        )\n        if component is None:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n                result_type,\n                self.match_threshold,\n                result_id,\n            )\n            # Render the fallback card server-side for SSE streaming\n            fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n                summary=summary,\n                data=result_data,\n                urgency=urgency,\n            )\n            return RenderOutcome(\n                rendered_html=fallback_html,\n                component_id=None,\n                card_fallback=True,\n                layout_bucket=bucket,\n            )\n\n        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n        self.library.cache_card(\n            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n        )\n        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n            result_id,\n            component.id,\n            component.version,\n            result_type,\n        )\n        return RenderOutcome(\n            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n            component_id=component.id,\n            card_fallback=False,\n            layout_bucket=bucket,\n        )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n\n    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not template:\n        return \"\"\n\n    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n        path = match.group(1).strip()\n        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n        if value is None:\n            return \"\"\n        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n\n    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n\n\ndef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n    value: Any = data\n    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n        if isinstance(value, dict):\n            value = value.get(key)\n        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n            value = value[int(key)]\n        else:\n            return None\n    return value\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n    if value is None:\n        return ''\n    if isinstance(value, str):\n        return value\n    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n        return str(value)\n    try:\n        return json.dumps(value)\n    except Exception:\n        return str(value)\n\n\ndef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n    if data is None:\n        return []\n\n    obj = data\n\n    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n    if isinstance(data, str):\n        try:\n            obj = json.loads(data)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            return [('value', data)]\n\n    # Handle array\n    if isinstance(obj, list):\n        rows = []\n        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle object\n    if isinstance(obj, dict):\n        rows = []\n        for k in list(obj.keys())[:50]:  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((k, _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle primitive\n    return [('value', str(obj))]\n\n\ndef render_fallback_card(\n    summary: str | None = None,\n    data: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    urgency: str | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Render the generic fallback card HTML (server-side version of createFallbackCard).\n\n    This generates the same HTML structure as the client-side createFallbackCard()\n    function in canvas.js, ensuring the fallback card can be streamed via SSE\n    and injected directly into the canvas without a blank canvas state.\n\n    All dynamic values are HTML-escaped per the escaping contract.\n\n    Args:\n        summary: Optional result summary\n        data: Optional result data dict for key/value grid\n        urgency: Optional urgency level for badge\n\n    Returns:\n        HTML string for the fallback card\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Build fallback card HTML\n        card_parts = ['<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\">']\n\n        # Header with icon and title\n        card_parts.append('  <div class=\"builtin-header\">')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-icon\">{html.escape(\"🗒️\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-title\">{html.escape(\"Result\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Summary paragraph\n        if summary:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary, quote=False)}</p>')\n\n        # Key/value grid from data\n        if data:\n            rows = _fallback_rows(data)\n            if rows:\n                card_parts.append('  <div class=\"fallback-grid\">')\n                for key, value in rows:\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-key\">{html.escape(key, quote=False)}</div>')\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-val\">{html.escape(value, quote=False)}</div>')\n                card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Urgency badge\n        if urgency:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <span class=\"urgency-badge {html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}\">{html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}</span>')\n\n        card_parts.append('</div>')\n\n        return '\\n'.join(card_parts)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        # Fallback render failures should not crash the dispatch flow\n        logger.error(f\"Fallback card render failed: {e}\")\n        # Return minimal safe fallback HTML\n        return f'<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\"><p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary or \"Result\", quote=False)}</p></div>'\n\n\ndef select_rendered_card(\n    result_type: str,\n    result_data: dict[str, Any],\n    result_id: str,\n    layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n) -> Optional[str]:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic component selector: (result_type, result.data) → Optional[rendered_html].\n\n    Pure SQL lookup — no LLM call in this path. Queries component_usage_patterns\n    for the highest match_score where result_type matches. If found and score above\n    threshold: fills component template with result.data, writes to card_cache, and\n    returns rendered HTML. If no match or below threshold: returns None (triggers\n    fallback rendering by the caller).\n\n    This is the server-side deterministic component selector specified in the task\n    acceptance criteria. It returns Optional[str] directly, unlike render() which\n    returns a RenderOutcome object with additional metadata.\n\n    Args:\n        result_type: The result type to match against component_usage_patterns\n        result_data: The result data dict to fill into the component template\n        result_id: The result ID for card_cache write\n        layout_bucket: The layout bucket to use (default: 'normal')\n        match_threshold: Minimum match score threshold (default: 0.7)\n        library: Optional ComponentLibrary instance (uses singleton if not provided)\n\n    Returns:\n        Rendered HTML string if a component matched, None if no match or below threshold\n    \"\"\"\n    lib = library or get_library()\n\n    # Query component_usage_patterns for highest match_score where result_type matches\n    component = lib.select_component_for_result_type(\n        result_type=result_type,\n        match_threshold=match_threshold,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n    )\n\n    # If no component matched or below threshold, return None (triggers fallback)\n    if component is None:\n        logger.debug(\n            \"select_rendered_card: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n            \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n            result_type,\n            match_threshold,\n            result_id,\n        )\n        return None\n\n    # On match: fill component template with result.data\n    rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n    # Write to card_cache\n    lib.cache_card(\n        result_id=result_id,\n        component_id=component.id,\n        component_version=component.version,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n        rendered_html=rendered_html,\n    )\n\n    # intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n51\t      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n52\t    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n53\t      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n54\t      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n55\t    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n56\t\n57\t    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n58\t    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n59\t    \"\"\"\n60\t    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n61\t    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n62\t    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n63\t        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n64\t    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n65\t        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n66\t    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n67\t\n68\t\n69\t@dataclass\n70\tclass RenderOutcome:\n71\t    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n72\t\n73\t    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n74\t    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n75\t    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n76\t    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n77\t    \"\"\"\n78\t\n79\t    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n80\t    component_id: Optional[str]\n81\t    card_fallback: bool\n82\t    layout_bucket: str\n83\t\n84\t\n85\tclass HotPathRenderer:\n86\t    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n87\t\n88\t    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n89\t    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n90\t    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n91\t    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n92\t    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n93\t    \"\"\"\n94\t\n95\t    def __init__(\n96\t        self,\n97\t        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n98\t        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n99\t        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n100\t    ):\n101\t        self.library = library or get_library()\n102\t        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n103\t        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n104\t\n105\t    def render(\n106\t        self,\n107\t        result_id: str,\n108\t        result_type: str,\n109\t        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n110\t        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n111\t        urgency: Optional[str] = None,\n112\t        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n113\t    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n114\t        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n115\t\n116\t        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n117\t        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n118\t        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n119\t        outcome with the rendered fallback HTML and writes nothing to the\n120\t        component DB.\n121\t        \"\"\"\n122\t        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n123\t\n124\t        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n125\t            result_type, self.match_threshold\n126\t        )\n127\t        if component is None:\n128\t            logger.debug(\n129\t                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n130\t                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n131\t                result_type,\n132\t                self.match_threshold,\n133\t                result_id,\n134\t            )\n135\t            # Render the fallback card server-side for SSE streaming\n136\t            fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n137\t                summary=summary,\n138\t                data=result_data,\n139\t                urgency=urgency,\n140\t            )\n141\t            return RenderOutcome(\n142\t                rendered_html=fallback_html,\n143\t                component_id=None,\n144\t                card_fallback=True,\n145\t                layout_bucket=bucket,\n146\t            )\n147\t\n148\t        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n149\t\n150\t        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n151\t        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n152\t        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n153\t        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n154\t        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n155\t        self.library.cache_card(\n156\t            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n157\t        )\n158\t        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n159\t        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n160\t        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n161\t\n162\t        logger.info(\n163\t            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n164\t            result_id,\n165\t            component.id,\n166\t            component.version,\n167\t            result_type,\n168\t        )\n169\t        return RenderOutcome(\n170\t            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n171\t            component_id=component.id,\n172\t            card_fallback=False,\n173\t            layout_bucket=bucket,\n174\t        )\n175\t\n176\t\n177\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n178\t# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n179\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n180\t\n181\tdef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n182\t    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n183\t\n184\t    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n185\t    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n186\t    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n187\t    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n188\t    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n189\t    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n190\t    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n191\t    \"\"\"\n192\t    if not template:\n193\t        return \"\"\n194\t\n195\t    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n196\t        path = match.group(1).strip()\n197\t        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n198\t        if value is None:\n199\t            return \"\"\n200\t        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n201\t\n202\t    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n203\t\n204\t\n205\tdef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n206\t    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n207\t    value: Any = data\n208\t    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n209\t        if isinstance(value, dict):\n210\t            value = value.get(key)\n211\t        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n212\t            value = value[int(key)]\n213\t        else:\n214\t            return None\n215\t    return value\n216\t\n217\t\n218\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n219\t# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n220\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n221\t\n222\tdef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n223\t    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n224\t    if value is None:\n225\t        return ''\n226\t    if isinstance(value, str):\n227\t        return value\n228\t    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n229\t        return str(value)\n230\t    try:\n231\t        return json.dumps(value)\n232\t    except Exception:\n233\t        return str(value)\n234\t\n235\t\n236\tdef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n237\t    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n238\t    if data is None:\n239\t        return []\n240\t\n241\t    obj = data\n242\t\n243\t    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n244\t    if isinstance(data, str):\n245\t        try:\n246\t            obj = json.loads(data)\n247\t        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n248\t            return [('value', data)]\n249\t\n250\t    # Handle array\n251\t    if isinstance(obj, list):\n252\t        rows = []\n253\t        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n254\t            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n255\t        return rows\n256\t\n257\t    # Handle object\n258\t    if isinstance(obj, dict):\n259\t        rows = []\n260\t        for k in list(obj.keys())[:50]:  # Max 50 rows\n261\t            rows.append((k, _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n262\t        return rows\n263\t\n264\t    # Handle primitive\n265\t    return [('value', str(obj))]\n266\t\n267\t\n268\tdef render_fallback_card(\n269\t    summary: str | None = None,\n270\t    data: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n271\t    urgency: str | None = None,\n272\t) -> str:\n273\t    \"\"\"Render the generic fallback card HTML (server-side version of SSE\n277\t    and injected directly into the canvas without a blank canvas state.\n278\t\n279\t    All dynamic values are HTML-escaped per the escaping contract.\n280\t\n281\t    Args:\n282\t        summary: Optional result summary\n283\t        data: Optional result data dict for key/value grid\n284\t        urgency: Optional urgency level for badge\n285\t\n286\t    Returns:\n287\t        HTML string for the fallback card\n288\t    \"\"\"\n289\t    try:\n290\t        # Build fallback card HTML\n291\t        card_parts = ['<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\">']\n292\t\n293\t        # Header with icon and title\n294\t        card_parts.append('  <div class=\"builtin-header\">')\n295\t        card_parts.append(f'    <span quote=False)}</p>')\n302\t\n303\t        # Key/value grid from data\n304\t        if data:\n305\t            rows = _fallback_rows(data)\n306\t            if rows:\n307\t                card_parts.append('  <div class=\"fallback-grid\">')\n308\t                for key, value in rows:\n309\t                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-key\">{html.escape(key, quote=False)}</div>')\n310\t                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-val\">{html.escape(value, quote=False)}</div>')\n311\t                card_parts.append(' quote=False)}</span>')\n316\t\n317\t        card_parts.append('</div>')\n318\t\n319\t        return '\\n'.join(card_parts)\n320\t\n321\t    except Exception as e:\n322\t        # Fallback render failures should not crash the dispatch flow\n323\t        logger.error(f\"Fallback card render failed: {e}\")\n324\t        # Return minimal safe fallback HTML\n325\t        return f'<div First state\n    initial_state = {\n        \"project_slug\": \"test-pipeline\",\n        \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n        \"phase\": \"Running\",\n        \"restarts\": 0,\n    }\n\n    # Set initial state in cache\n    await ambient_monitor._update_topic_context_cache(\n        sample_monitoring_rule.topic_id,\n        initial_state,\n    )\n\n    # First check - should establish baseline and return False\n    has_change, changes_dict = await ambient_monitor.detect_state_change(\n        sample_monitoring_rule,\n        current_state,\n    )\n\n    assert has_change is False, \"First check should establish baseline, not notify\"\n    assert changes_dict[\"is_first\"] is True\n\n    # Set initial state\n    initial_state = {\"phase\": \"Running\", \"restarts\": 0, \"ready\": \"1/1\"}\n    await ambient_monitor._update_topic_context_cache(\n        sample_monitoring_rule.topic_id,\n        initial_state,\n    )\n\n    # Test with any_change threshold\n    sample_monitoring_rule.notification_threshold = \"any_change\"\n\n    # Same state - no change\n    has_change, changes_dict = await ambient_monitor.detect_state_change(\n        sample_monitoring_rule,\n        initial_state,\n    )\n    assert has_change is False\n\n    # Any field change - should trigger\n    changed_state = {\"phase\": \"Running\", \"restarts\": 1, \"ready\": \"1/1\"}\n    has_change, changes_dict = await ambient_monitor.detect_state_change(\n        sample_monitoring_rule,\n        changed_state,\n    )\n    assert has_change is True\n    assert \"restarts\" in changes_dict.get(\"changed_fields\", [])\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_state_change_detection_state_change_threshold(ambient_monitor, sample_monitoring_rule):\n    \"\"\"Test state change detection with notification_threshold='state_change'.\"\"\"\n    # Set initial state\n    initial_state = {\n        \"phase\": \"Running\",\n        \"status\": \"Healthy\",\n        \"restarts\": 0,\n        \"other_field\": \"value\",\n    }\n    await ambient_monitor._update_topic_context_cache(\n        sample_monitoring_rule.topic_id,\n        initial_state,\n    )\n\n    # Test various state change scenarios\n453\t    test_cases = [\n454\t        {\n455\t            \"current\": {\"phase\": \"Failed\", \"restarts\": 0},\n456\t            \"previous\": {\"phase\": \"Running\", \"restarts\": 0},\n457\t            \"changed_fields\": [\"phase\"],\n458\t            \"diff\": {\"phase\": {\"from\": \"Running\", \"to\": \"Failed\"}},\n459\t            \"expected_keywords\": [\"phase\", \"changed\", \"Failed\"],\n460\t        },\n461\t        {\n462\t            \"current\": {\"phase\": \"Running\", \"restarts\": 6},\n463\t            \"previous\": {\"phase\": \"Running\", \"restarts\": 0},\n464\t            \"changed_fields\": [\"restarts\"],\n465\t            \"diff\": {\"restarts\": {\"from\": 0, \"to\": 6}},\n466\t            \"expected_keywords\": [\"changed\", \"restarts\"],\n467\t        },\n468\t        {\n469\t            \"current\": {\"sync_status\": \"OutOfSync\", \"phase\": \"Running\"},\n470\t            \"previous\": {\"sync_status\": \"Synced\", \"phase\": \"Running\"},\n471\t            \"changed_fields\": [\"sync_status\"],\n472\t            \"diff\": {\"sync_status\": {\"from\": \"Synced\", \"to\": \"OutOfSync\"}},\n473\t            \"expected_keywords\": [\"sync\", \"OutOfSync\"],\n474\t        },\n475\t    ]\n476\t\n477\t    for test_case in test_cases:\n478\t        changes_dict = {\n479\t            \"is_first\": False,\n480\t            \"changed_fields\": test_case[\"changed_fields\"],\n481\t            \"diff\": test_case[\"diff\"],\n482\t        }\n483\t\n484\t        summary = ambient_monitor._generate_summary(\n485\t            rule=sample_monitoring_rule,\n486\t            current_state=test_case[\"current\"],\n487\t            previous_state=test_case[\"previous\"],\n488\t            changes_dict=changes_dict,\n489\t        )\n490\t\n491\t        # Verify summary is a non-empty string\n492\t        assert isinstance(summary, str)\n493\t        assert len(summary) > 0\n494\t\n495\t        # Verify summary contains expected keywords (deterministic based on change)\n496\t        for keyword in test_case[\"expected_keywords\"]:\n497\t            # Test various state change scenarios\n    test_cases = [\n        {\n            \"current\": {\"phase\": \"Failed\", \"restarts\": 0},\n            \"previous\": {\"phase\": \"Running\", \"restarts\": 0},\n            \"changed_fields\": [\"phase\"],\n            \"diff\": {\"phase\": {\"from\": \"Running\", \"to\": \"Failed\"}},\n            \"expected_keywords\": [\"phase\", \"changed\", \"Failed\"],\n        },\n        {\n            \"current\": {\"phase\": \"Running\", \"restarts\": 6},\n            \"previous\": {\"phase\": \"Running\", \"restarts\": 0},\n            \"changed_fields\": [\"restarts\"],\n            \"diff\": {\"restarts\": {\"from\": 0, \"to\": 6}},\n            \"expected_keywords\": [\"changed\", \"restarts\"],\n        },\n        {\n            \"current\": {\"sync_status\": \"OutOfSync\", \"phase\": \"Running\"},\n            \"previous\": {\"sync_status\": \"Synced\", \"phase\": \"Running\"},\n            \"changed_fields\": [\"sync_status\"],\n            \"diff\": {\"sync_status\": {\"from\": \"Synced\", \"to\": \"OutOfSync\"}},\n            \"expected_keywords\": [\"sync\", \"OutOfSync\"],\n        },\n    ]\n\n    for test_case in test_cases:\n        changes_dict = {\n            \"is_first\": False,\n            \"changed_fields\": test_case[\"changed_fields\"],\n            \"diff\": test_case[\"diff\"],\n        }\n\n        summary = ambient_monitor._generate_summary(\n            rule=sample_monitoring_rule,\n            current_state=test_case[\"current\"],\n            previous_state=test_case[\"previous\"],\n            changes_dict=changes_dict,\n        )\n\n        # Verify summary is a non-empty string\n        assert isinstance(summary, str)\n        assert len(summary) > 0\n\n        # Verify summary contains expected keywords (deterministic based on change)\n        for keyword in test_case[\"expected_keywords\"]:\n            #
