How Ambient Monitoring Fetches and Diffs State\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/monitoring/ambient.py`\n\nThe ambient monitoring system follows a polling-based architecture:\n\n### State Fetching (`check_topic_state` method, lines 165-222)\n- Uses the **fetch orchestrator** with the full command matrix to get current state\n- Maps monitoring intent types to fetch IntentTypes (e.g., \"status\" → `IntentType.STATUS`)\n- Creates `FetchRequest` objects with `FetchContext` containing project-specific details\n- Executes fetch via `execute_fetch(request)` which calls the orchestrator\n- Extracts successful data from multiple fetch sources\n- Applies filters if specified in the monitoring rule\n\n### State Diffing (`detect_state_change` method, lines 311-361)\n- **First check behavior**: No notification on first check - only caches baseline state\n- **Subsequent checks**: Computes diff between cached previous state and current state\n- **Diff computation**: Uses `_compute_diff()` method (lines 457-471) to compare field-by-field\n- **Change detection**: Supports two notification thresholds:\n  - `\"any_change\"`: Any field change triggers notification\n  - `\"state_change\"`: Only significant state fields (phase, status, health, ready, sync_status) trigger notification\n\n### State Caching (`topic_context_cache` table)\n- **Purpose**: Stores previous state for diff comparison\n- **TTL**: 10 minutes default (configurable via `TOPIC_CONTEXT_TTL_SECONDS`)\n- **Storage**: JSON-serialized state data in `topic_context_cache` table\n- **Cache update**: Always updated after each check (even when no change detected)\n\n## 2. How the Rule Engine Works and When Rules Fire\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/monitoring/ambient.py` (lines 508-593)\n\n### Rule Engine Architecture\n\n**Exception Rules (`evaluate_exception_rules` method, lines 508-543)**\n- **Input**: Changes dict with `changed_fields` list and `diff` dictionary\n- **Firing condition**: Rules ONLY fire when matching state changes occur in the diff\n- **No firing**: Returns empty list when no relevant state changes\n- **Project filtering**: Rules can be project-specific or global (`project_slug=None`)\n\n**Rule Condition Matching (`_rule_condition_matches` method, lines 545-593)**\n- **Condition format**: Simple field comparisons (`phase==Failed`, `restarts>5`, `sync_status!=Synced`)\n- **Supported operators**: `==`, `!=`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`\n- **Evaluation logic**: Checks if the changed field matches the condition against the **new value** (`diff[field][\"to\"]`)\n- **Type handling**: Converts values to float for numeric comparisons\n\n**Rule Output Format**\n```python\n{\n    \"project_slug\": \"options-pipeline\",\n    \"urgency\": \"high\", \n    \"exception_type\": \"pod-crash-loop\",\n    \"condition\": \"restart_count > 5\",\n    \"message\": \"Pod is in crash loop\"\n}\n```\n\n### When Rules Fire\n1. **State change must occur**: Rule evaluation only happens after `detect_state_change` returns `True`\n2. **Field must match**: Only checks fields in the `diff` (changed fields only)\n3. **Condition must be met**: New value must satisfy the rule's condition\n4. **Project must match** (unless `project_slug=None`): Rules filter by project slug\n\n## 3. Where Results Should Be Persisted\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/session/store.py`\n\n### Database Schema (`results` table, lines 84-116)\n\n**Key columns for monitoring results:**\n- **`intent_id`**: NULL for monitoring-originated results (line 86)\n- **`topic_id`**: Links to the topic being monitored (line 87)\n- **`session_id`**: Session that receives the monitoring result (line 88)\n- **`result_type`**: Format `\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"` (line 92)\n- **`card_fallback`**: 1 if generic fallback card used, 0 if component rendered (line 99)\n- **`data`**: JSON blob containing monitoring-specific structure (line 90)\n\n### Result Creation (`push_monitoring_result` method, lines 363-456)\n\n**Result Data Structure:**\n```python\n{\n    \"monitoring\": True,\n    \"current_state\": {...},\n    \"previous_state\": {...}, \n    \"changed_fields\": [\"phase\", \"restarts\"],\n    \"diff\": {\"phase\": {\"from\": \"Running\", \"to\": \"Failed\"}},\n    \"is_first_check\": False,\n    \"rule_filters\": [...],\n    \"notification_threshold\": \"any_change\",\n    \"triggered_rules\": [...]  # Exception rules that fired\n}\n```\n\n**Creation Process:**\n1. Find or create topic via `find_or_create_topic()`\n2. Generate deterministic summary via `_generate_summary()` (no LLM call)\n3. Derive `result_type` via `derive_result_type(intent_type=\"monitoring\", project_slug=...)`\n4. Call `store.create_result()` with `intent_id=None`\n5. Render card via hot-path renderer (deterministic component selection)\n6. Update `card_fallback` flag based on render outcome\n7. Broadcast SSE event\n\n### Key Persistence Points\n- **Results table**: Primary storage for all monitoring results\n- **Topic context cache**: Stores state between checks for diff computation\n- **Card cache**: Server-side rendered HTML for performance\n\n## 4. How SSE Broadcasting Works\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/sse/broadcaster.py`\n\n### SSE Architecture (`SSEBroadcaster` class, lines 84-283)\n\n**Connection Management:**\n- **Registration**: `register()` creates `SSEConnection` with unique `connection_id` (lines 111-127)\n- **Per-connection queues**: Each connection has its own `asyncio.Queue` for event delivery (line 59)\n- **Keepalive system**: Sends `\": ping\\n\\n\"` comments every 5 seconds to detect network drops (lines 33-35, 216-222)\n- **Cleanup loop**: Removes dead connections after 5 minutes of inactivity (lines 253-283)\n\n**Event Broadcasting (`broadcast` method, lines 165-189)**\n- **Targeting filters**: \n  - `target_session_id`: Only send to specific session\n  - `target_surface_id`: Only send to specific surface\n  - `exclude_surface_id`: Exclude specific surface\n- **Queue-based delivery**: Events are queued per-connection (non-blocking put)\n- **Return value**: Number of connections event was sent to\n\n**Event Streaming (`event_generator` method, lines 191-248)**\n- **Generator pattern**: Yields SSE-formatted messages for Flask/streaming response\n- **Initial event**: Sends \"connected\" event with connection details (lines 199-203)\n- **Event processing**: Waits for queue events with keepalive timeout (lines 216-222)\n- **Special handling**: `_DROP` sentinel causes abrupt disconnect (forces browser reconnect)\n\n**SSE Event Format (`_format_sse` method, lines 249-251)**\n```\nevent: result_created\ndata: {\"id\": \"...\", \"summary\": \"...\", ...}\n\n```\n\n### Monitoring-Specific Broadcasting (`broadcast_result` function, lines 337-362)\n\n**Monitoring Result Event Structure:**\n```python\nSSEEvent(\n    event_type=\"result_created\",\n    data={\n        \"id\": result_id,\n        \"intent_id\": None,  # NULL for monitoring\n        \"result_type\": \"monitoring:options-pipeline\",\n        \"summary\": \"Phase changed from Running to Failed\",\n        \"urgency\": \"high\",\n        \"rendered_html\": \"<div>...</div>\",  # Pre-rendered card\n        \"card_fallback\": False,\n        ...\n    },\n    target_session_id=session_id,\n    rendered_html=\"<div>...</div>\"  # For canvas injection\n)\n```\n\n**Broadcast Flow:**\n1. How Ambient Monitoring Fetches and Diffs State\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/monitoring/ambient.py`\n\nThe ambient monitoring system follows a polling-based architecture:\n\n### State Fetching (`check_topic_state` method, lines 165-222)\n- Uses the **fetch orchestrator** with the full command matrix to get current state\n- Maps monitoring intent types to fetch IntentTypes (e.g., \"status\" → `IntentType.STATUS`)\n- Creates `FetchRequest` objects with `FetchContext` containing project-specific details\n- Executes fetch via `execute_fetch(request)` which calls the orchestrator\n- Extracts successful data from multiple fetch sources\n- Applies filters if specified in the monitoring rule\n\n### State Diffing (`detect_state_change` method, lines 311-361)\n- **First check behavior**: No notification on first check - only caches baseline state\n- **Subsequent checks**: Computes diff between cached previous state and current state\n- **Diff computation**: Uses `_compute_diff()` method (lines 457-471) to compare field-by-field\n- **Change detection**: Supports two notification thresholds:\n  - `\"any_change\"`: Any field change triggers notification\n  - `\"state_change\"`: Only significant state fields (phase, status, health, ready, sync_status) trigger notification\n\n### State Caching (`topic_context_cache` table)\n- **Purpose**: Stores previous state for diff comparison\n- **TTL**: 10 minutes default (configurable via `TOPIC_CONTEXT_TTL_SECONDS`)\n- **Storage**: JSON-serialized state data in `topic_context_cache` table\n- **Cache update**: Always updated after each check (even when no change detected)\n\n## 2. Set up a config with exception rules\n    monitor.config = MonitoringConfig(\n        active_topics=[],\n        exceptions=[\n            ExceptionRule(\n                name=\"pod_failure\",\n                project_slug=\"test-pipeline\",\n                condition=\"phase==Failed\",\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                message=\"Pod has failed\",\n            ),\n            ExceptionRule(\n                name=\"high_restart_count\",\n                project_slug=\"test-pipeline\",\n                condition=\"restarts>5\",\n                urgency=\"normal\",\n                message=\"Pod has high restart count\",\n            ),\n        ],\n        batching={},\n        quiet_hours={},\n        channels={},\n    )\n\n    return monitor\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef sample_monitoring_rule():\n    \"\"\"Create a sample monitoring rule for testing.\"\"\"\n    return MonitoringRule(\n        topic_id=\"test-pipeline-status\",\n        project_slug=\"test-pipeline\",\n        intent_type=\"status\",\n        check_interval=60,\n        urgency=\"normal\",\n        filters=[],\n        notification_threshold=\"any_change\",\n    )\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\nasync def session_with_surface(store):\n    \"\"\"Create a session with an active surface for testing.\"\"\"\n    session_id = \"test-session\"\n    await store.create_session(session_id)\n    surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n    return session_id, surface_id\n\n\n# --- Integration Tests ----------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\n@patch(\"src.sse.broadcaster.get_broadcaster\")\nasync def test_state_change_rule_fire_result_row_sse_event_full_flow(\n    mock_get_broadcaster,\n    ambient_monitor,\n    sample_monitoring_rule,\n    store,\n    session_with_surface,\n    mock_broadcaster,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Integration test: state change → rule fire → result row → SSE event.\n\n    Verifies the complete flow:\n    1. Initial state is cached (no result)\n    2. State change occurs\n    3. Rule fires (phase==Failed condition matches)\n    4. Result row is written with correct structure\n    5. SSE event is broadcast\n    6. Result is retrievable from database\n    \"\"\"\n    # Setup the mock broadcaster\n    mock_get_broadcaster.return_value = mock_broadcaster\n\n    session_id, surface_id = session_with_surface\n\n    # Step 1: Set initial state (baseline)\n    initial_state = {\"phase\": \"Running\", \"restarts\": 0}\n    await ambient_monitor._update_topic_context_cache(\n        sample_monitoring_rule.topic_id,\n        initial_state,\n    )\n\n    # Verify no results exist yet\n    session_results = [r for r in await store.get_all_results() if r[\"session_id\"] == session_id]\n    assert len(session_results) == 0, \"No results should exist before state change\"\n\n    # Step 2: Simulate state change (phase goes to Failed)\n    current_state = {\"phase\": \"Failed\", \"restarts\": 3}\n    has_change, changes_dict = await ambient_monitor.detect_state_change(\n        sample_monitoring_rule,\n        current_state,\n    )\n\n    assert has_change is True, \"State change should be detected\"\n    assert changes_dict[\"is_first\"] is False\n    assert \"phase\" in changes_dict[\"changed_fields\"]\n\n    # Step 3: Evaluate rules (phase==Failed should trigger pod_failure rule)\n    triggered_rules = ambient_monitor.evaluate_exception_rules(\n        changes_dict=changes_dict,\n        project_slug=sample_monitoring_rule.project_slug,\n        rules=ambient_monitor.config.exceptions,\n    )\n\n    assert len(triggered_rules) == 1, \"pod_failure rule should fire\"\n    assert triggered_rules[0][\"exception_type\"] == \"pod_failure\"\n    assert triggered_rules[0][\"urgency\"] == \"high\"\n\n    # Add triggered rules to changes dict (as monitor_topic does)\n    changes_dict[\"triggered_rules\"] = triggered_rules\n\n    # Step 4: Push monitoring result (this writes to DB and broadcasts SSE)\n    # Call push_monitoring_result\n    await ambient_monitor.push_monitoring_result(\n        rule=sample_monitoring_rule,\n        current_state=current_state,\n        changes_dict=changes_dict,\n        session_id=session_id,\n    )\n\n    # Step 5: Verify result row was written with correct structure\n    session_results = [r for r in await store.get_all_results() if r[\"session_id\"] == session_id]\n    assert len(session_results) == 1, \"One result should be created\"\n\n    result = session_results[0]\n\n    # Verify intent_id is NULL (monitoring-originated)\n    assert result[\"intent_id\"] is None, \"intent_id should be NULL for monitoring results\"\n\n    # Verify topic_id is set\n    assert result[\"topic_id\"] is not None, \"topic_id should be set\"\n\n    # Verify result_type follows 'monitoring:{project_slug}' format\n    expected_result_type = derive_result_type(\n        intent_type=\"monitoring\",\n        project_slug=sample_monitoring_rule.project_slug\n    )\n    assert result[\"result_type\"] == expected_result_type\n    assert result[\"result_type\"] == \"monitoring:test-pipeline\"\n\n    # Verify summary is deterministic template (no LLM)\n    assert result[\"summary\"] is not None\n    assert \"phase changed\" in result[\"summary\"].lower() or \"failed\" in result[\"summary\"].lower()\n    # Summary should mention the state change\n    assert \"Failed\" in result[\"summary\"] or \"failed\" in result[\"summary\"]\n\n    # Verify urgency\n    assert result[\"urgency\"] == sample_monitoring_rule.urgency\n\n    # Verify data contains monitoring fields\n    import json\n    result_data = json.loads(result[\"data\"])\n    assert result_data[\"monitoring\"] is True\n    assert \"current_state\" in result_data\n    assert \"previous_state\" in result_data\n    assert result_data[\"current_state\"][\"phase\"] == \"Failed\"\n    assert result_data[\"previous_state\"][\"phase\"] == \"Running\"\n    assert result_data[\"changed_fields\"] == [\"phase\", \"restarts\"]\n    assert len(result_data.get(\"triggered_rules\", [])) == 1\n    assert result_data[\"triggered_rules\"][0][\"exception_type\"] == \"pod_failure\"\n\n    # Step 6: Verify SSE event was broadcast\n    assert mock_broadcaster.broadcast.called, \"broadcast() should be called\"\n    assert mock_broadcaster.broadcast.call_count == 1\n\n    # Get the SSE event that was broadcast\n    call_args = mock_broadcaster.broadcast.call_args\n    # The first positional argument is the SSEEvent\n    sse_event = call_args[0][0]\n\n    assert isinstance(sse_event, SSEEvent), \"broadcast() should receive SSEEvent\"\n    assert sse_event.event_type == \"result_created\"\n    assert sse_event.data[\"id\"] == result[\"id\"]\n    assert sse_event.data[\"intent_id\"] is None\n    assert sse_event.data[\"result_type\"] == \"monitoring:test-pipeline\"\n    assert sse_event.data[\"urgency\"] == \"normal\"\n    assert sse_event.target_session_id == session_id\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_no_result_when_rule_does_not_fire(\n    ambient_monitor,\n    sample_monitoring_rule,\n    store,\n    session_with_surface,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that no result is created when rule doesn't fire (no matching state change).\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id, surface_id = session_with_surface\n\n    # Set initial state\n    initial_state = {\"phase\": \"Running\", \"restarts\": 0}\n    await ambient_monitor._update_topic_context_cache(\n        sample_monitoring_rule.topic_id,\n        initial_state,\n    )\n\n    # State change that doesn't trigger any rule (phase stays Running)\n    current_state = {\"phase\": \"Running\", \"restarts\": 2}\n    has_change, changes_dict = await ambient_monitor.detect_state_change(\n        sample_monitoring_rule,\n        current_state,\n    )\n\n    assert has_change is True, \"State change should be detected\"\n\n    # Evaluate rules - none should fire (no phase==Failed, no restarts>5)\n    triggered_rules = ambient_monitor.evaluate_exception_rules(\n        changes_dict=changes_dict,\n        project_slug=sample_monitoring_rule.project_slug,\n        rules=ambient_monitor.config.exceptions,\n    )\n\n    assert len(triggered_rules) == 0, \"No rules should fire\"\n\n    # Push result anyway (normal state change result, no exception rules triggered)\n    await ambient_monitor.push_monitoring_result(\n        rule=sample_monitoring_rule,\n        current_state=current_state,\n        changes_dict=changes_dict,\n        session_id=session_id,\n    )\n\n    # Verify result was still created (state change results are always written)\n    session_results = [r for r in await store.get_all_results() if r[\"session_id\"] == session_id]\n    assert len(session_results) == 1, \"Result should be created even when no exception rules fire\"\n\n    # But no triggered_rules in data\n    result = session_results[0]\n    import json\n    result_data = json.loads(result[\"data\"])\n    assert len(result_data.get(\"triggered_rules\", [])) == 0\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_multiple_rules_fire_on_single_state_change(\n    ambient_monitor,\n    sample_monitoring_rule,\n    store,\n    session_with_surface,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that multiple rules can fire on a single state change and all are captured.\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id, surface_id = session_with_surface\n\n    # Add an additional rule that also matches phase==Failed\n    ambient_monitor.config.exceptions.append(\n        ExceptionRule(\n            name=\"critical_failure\",\n            project_slug=\"test-pipeline\",\n            condition=\"phase==Failed\",\n            urgency=\"critical\",\n            message=\"Critical failure detected\",\n        ),\n    )\n\n    # Set initial state\n    initial_state = {\"phase\": \"Running\", \"restarts\": 0}\n    await ambient_monitor._update_topic_context_cache(\n        sample_monitoring_rule.topic_id,\n        initial_state,\n    )\n\n    # State change that triggers both rules\n    current_state = {\"phase\": \"Failed\", \"restarts\": 10}  # Also triggers restarts>5\n    has_change, changes_dict = await ambient_monitor.detect_state_change(\n        sample_monitoring_rule,\n        current_state,\n    )\n\n    # Evaluate rules - multiple should fire\n    triggered_rules = ambient_monitor.evaluate_exception_rules(\n        changes_dict=changes_dict,\n        project_slug=sample_monitoring_rule.project_slug,\n        rules=ambient_monitor.config.exceptions,\n    )\n\n    assert len(triggered_rules) == 3, \"Three rules should fire (pod_failure, critical_failure, high_restart_count)\"\n\n    exception_types = {r[\"exception_type\"] for r in triggered_rules}\n    assert \"pod_failure\" in exception_types\n    assert \"critical_failure\" in exception_types\n    assert \"high_restart_count\" in exception_types\n\n    # Push result with triggered rules\n    changes_dict[\"triggered_rules\"] = triggered_rules\n    await ambient_monitor.push_monitoring_result(\n        rule=sample_monitoring_rule,\n        current_state=current_state,\n        changes_dict=changes_dict,\n        session_id=session_id,\n    )\n\n    #
